title: Cell Dimension Reduction Plot — CellDimPlot
text: function extracts cell embeddings metadata input object, combines single data frame, passes result plotthis::DimPlot() along additional arguments provided via .... extraction logic varies object type: Seurat objects: Embeddings(object, reduction) provides coordinates; object@meta.data provides metadata. group_by NULL, Idents(object) used default grouping variable (column name "Identity"). graph parameter references object@graphs. Giotto objects: getDimReduction() output = "matrix" provides coordinates; getCellMetadata() output = "data.table" provides metadata. spat_unit feat_type resolved defaults specified. graph parameter references nearest-neighbor networks retrieved via getNearestNetwork() converted igraph. h5ad files: obsm[[reduction]] provides embedding matrix; obs provides metadata (decoded via h5group_to_dataframe() categorical variables). graph parameter references obsp[["connectivities"]], converted via igraph adjacency matrix.

title: Cell Dimension Reduction Plot — CellDimPlot
text: velocity specified, velocity vectors overlaid dimension reduction plot. velocity parameter names second reduction (e.g., "stochastic_UMAP") whose first two dimensions encode velocity arrows. lightweight overlay — dedicated velocity visualization grid stream plots, use CellVelocityPlot delegates plotthis::VelocityPlot().

title: Cell Dimension Reduction Plot — CellDimPlot
Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: Visualizes cell-level statistics — counts, fractions, composition — across cell identities metadata groupings. primary function exploring distribution cell types, clusters, categorical metadata single-cell transcriptomics datasets. answers questions : "proportion cell type condition?", "cluster abundances change across samples?", "clonal composition within cell type?" CellStatPlot serves unified interface across 15+ visualization types, driven common data aggregation fraction-calculation pipeline. supports four single-cell data containers: Seurat objects — Extracts @meta.data; uses Idents() default identity ident = NULL. Giotto objects — Extracts cell metadata via getCellMetadata() using spat_unit feat_type. h5ad files (.h5ad opened H5File) — Reads obs via h5group_to_dataframe(). Data frames — Internal method; methods ultimately delegate metadata extraction.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
code: CellStatPlot(   object,   ident = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   group_by_sep = "_",   spat_unit = NULL,   feat_type = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   facet_by = NULL,   rows_by = NULL,   columns_split_by = NULL,   frac = c("none", "group", "ident", "cluster", "all"),   name = NULL,   agg = "n()",   plot_type = c("bar", "circos", "pie", "pies", "ring", "donut", "trend", "area",     "sankey", "alluvial", "heatmap", "radar", "spider", "violin", "box"),   swap = FALSE,   ylab = NULL,   ... )

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: object Seurat object, Giotto object, path .h5ad file, opened H5File hdf5r package, data frame (internal method) containing cell metadata. ident metadata column containing cell identities (clusters, cell types). NULL Seurat object, active identity used stored column name "Identity". Required Giotto h5ad objects. column forms primary categorical axis plot — typically x-axis, pie slices, heatmap rows. group_by metadata column(s) used secondary grouping (conditions, samples, stimulation status). Default NULL. Behavior varies plot type: plots, multiple columns concatenated single grouping variable (separated group_by_sep). "sankey" "heatmap" plots, multiple columns concatenated — column forms separate node column grouping. "violin" "box" plots, 2 columns allowed: first determines x-axis breakdown, second passed group_by underlying plot function. group_by_sep Separator used concatenating multiple group_by columns single variable. Default "_". Ignored "sankey" "heatmap" plots columns combined. spat_unit Spatial unit name Giotto objects (e.g., "cell"). Ignored non-Giotto inputs. NULL, auto-detected via GiottoClass::set_default_spat_unit(). feat_type Feature type name Giotto objects (e.g., "rna", "dna", "protein"). Ignored non-Giotto inputs. NULL, auto-detected via GiottoClass::set_default_feat_type(). split_by Metadata column(s) used split data separate plots. unique value (combination) produces independent plot. Multiple columns concatenated using split_by_sep. Default NULL (splitting). split_by_sep Separator used concatenating multiple split_by columns. Default "_". facet_by Metadata column(s) used facet plots (separate panels within output). available "circos", "sankey", "heatmap" plot types. Default NULL. rows_by Metadata column(s) used rows "heatmap" "pies" plots. "pies", defines pie chart represents (e.g., clones). "heatmap", overrides default row grouping (otherwise ident). multiple columns provided, must logical numeric. Default NULL. columns_split_by Metadata column used split columns "heatmap" "pies" plots. adds additional level column faceting beyond group_by provides. Default NULL. frac fraction normalization mode. One "none" (default), "group", "ident", "cluster" (alias "ident"), "". Fractions calculated within split_by facet_by subset. See Fraction calculation section detailed semantics. name Display name main legend / value scale "heatmap" "pies" plots. Default NULL, auto-generates name based fraction mode. agg expression string passed dplyr::summarise() compute value ident--group intersection. Default "n()" (count cells). example, "sum(hasTCR) / n()" computes fraction TCR-positive cells. Ignored "circos" "pies" plot types. See Custom aggregation section examples. plot_type visualization type. One : "bar" (default), "circos", "pie", "pies", "ring"/"donut", "trend", "area", "sankey"/"alluvial", "heatmap", "radar", "spider", "violin", "box". See Plot types section guidance choosing type. Note: "donut" alias "ring"; "alluvial" alias "sankey". swap Whether exchange roles ident group_by. Default FALSE. Behavior plot type: "bar", "trend", "area", "ring", "radar", "spider": Puts group_by x-axis uses ident fill/color. "circos": Reverses direction edges (ident group_by instead group_by ident). "pies": Swaps group_by ident column grouping pie grouping respectively. "heatmap": Swaps group_by columns_split_by (requires columns_split_by set). "violin", "box": Swaps x-axis group_by assignments two group_by columns. works group_by specified. Note swap different flip (transposes coordinates). ylab Y-axis label. Default NULL, auto-generates label based fraction mode (e.g., "Number cells" "Fraction cells"). ... Additional arguments passed underlying plotthis plotting function. target function depends plot_type: "bar" — plotthis::BarPlot() (position, palette, alpha, x_text_angle, ...) "circos" — plotthis::CircosPlot() (labels_rot, links_alpha, ...) "pie" — plotthis::PieChart() "ring" / "donut" — plotthis::RingPlot() (palette, alpha, ...) "trend" — plotthis::TrendPlot() (palette, point_size, line_width, ...) "area" — plotthis::AreaPlot() (palette, alpha, ...) "sankey" / "alluvial" — plotthis::SankeyPlot() (links_alpha, links_fill_by, ...) "heatmap" — plotthis::Heatmap() (palette, cluster_rows, cluster_columns, show_row_names, show_column_names, cell_type, ...) "pies" — plotthis::Heatmap() (cell_type = "pie"; pie_size, pie_values, row_names_side, column_names_side, ...) "radar" — plotthis::RadarPlot() "spider" — plotthis::SpiderPlot() "violin" — plotthis::ViolinPlot() (add_box, comparisons, aspect.ratio, ...) "box" — plotthis::BoxPlot() (comparisons, aspect.ratio, ...) Common layout parameters (split_by, facet_by, combine, nrow, ncol) handled CellStatPlot directly.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: ggplot object, list ggplot objects combine = FALSE passed via ....

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: See: https://pwwang.github.io/scplotter/articles/Giotto_Xenium.html examples using function Giotto object. see: https://pwwang.github.io/scplotter/articles/Working_with_anndata_h5ad_files.html examples using function .h5ad files.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: Default y-axis label: ylab = NULL, label auto-generated "Number cells" (frac = "none") "Fraction cells" (otherwise). Override ylab custom labels. Heatmap/pies metadata columns: multiple rows_by columns specified "heatmap" "pies" plots, column must logical numeric. function filters cells column TRUE greater zero. Facet restrictions: facet_by supported "circos", "sankey", "heatmap" plot types. heatmaps, use split_by create separate plots. Factor ordering: order categories plots follows factor levels metadata columns. Set factor levels plotting control display order cell types groups. Giotto spatial units feature types: spat_unit feat_type parameters required locate correct metadata within Giotto's hierarchical spatial data structure. NULL, Giotto's default resolution used.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: plot_type parameter selects visualization. Plot types fall several conceptual categories: Composition within groups (requires group_by): "bar" — Grouped stacked bar chart. default common choice. Shows count fraction per ident, colored group. "trend" — Line chart connecting points across idents. Best showing trends across ordered categories (e.g., pseudotime bins, dose levels). "area" — Stacked area chart. Similar trend emphasizes cumulative composition. "ring" / "donut" — Ring (donut) chart. radial alternative stacked bars. frac forced "group". "radar" — Radar chart showing ident axis. Compact comparing group profiles across multiple idents. "spider" — Spider chart filled polygons. Similar radar emphasizes area covered group. Flow categories (requires group_by): "circos" — Circos plot showing directed edges one category another (e.g., cluster condition). "sankey" / "alluvial" — Sankey (alluvial) diagram multi-step categorical flows. Supports multiple group_by columns show cascading relationships. Single-group composition (group_by): "pie" — Pie chart cell counts per ident. Use quick overview cluster proportions. Matrix views (requires group_by): "heatmap" — Heatmap counts fractions idents rows group_by categories columns. Supports row splitting via rows_split_by column splitting via columns_split_by. "pies" — Heatmap cell pie chart showing sub-composition (e.g., clone distribution within cluster--condition intersection). Requires rows_by. Distribution within idents (requires group_by): "violin" — Violin plot showing distribution values per ident, grouped split metadata. "box" — Box plot structure violin.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: frac parameter controls cell counts normalized: "none" — Raw cell counts (default). y-axis shows absolute number cells. "group" — Fraction within group. total across idents within group sums 1. Answers: "cells condition X, fraction cell type?" "ident" — Fraction within ident. total across groups within ident sums 1. Answers: "Beta cells, fraction condition?" Requires group_by. "cluster" — Alias "ident". "" — Fraction cells data (split/facet subset). Answers: "fraction cells Beta cells condition X?" Fractions calculated independently within split_by /facet_by subset.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: default, CellStatPlot counts number cells ident--group intersection (agg = "n()"). agg parameter accepts expression can passed dplyr::summarise(), enabling custom metrics. Examples: "sum(hasTCR) / n()" — Fraction cells TCR group "mean(expression_score)" — Mean numeric metadata column "sum(clone_size > 1)" — Number expanded clones Note: agg ignored "circos" "pies" plot types, always count cells.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
text: CellStatPlot built around two categorical axes: ident — primary cell identity (clusters, cell types). typically forms x-axis, pie slices, heatmap rows. group_by — secondary grouping (conditions, samples, time points). typically forms fill colors, bar stacks, heatmap columns. swap parameter exchanges two roles: swap = TRUE, group_by variable used x-axis ident provides fill colors. exact behavior varies plot type — see swap parameter documentation details.

title: Cell statistics plot — CellStatPlot
code: # \donttest{ # library(patchwork) data(ifnb_sub)  # Bar plot p1 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub) p2 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim") p3 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", palette = "Set2") p4 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", position = "stack") (p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)   # Fraction of cells p1 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", frac = "group",                    ident = "seurat_annotations", x_text_angle = 60) p2 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", frac = "group") p3 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", frac = "ident",                    position = "stack", alpha = .6) p4 <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", frac = "group",                    swap = TRUE, position = "stack") (p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4)   # Splitting/Facetting the plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, split_by = "stim")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, facet_by = "stim")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, facet_by = "stim", facet_nrow = 2)   # Circos plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", plot_type = "circos")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", ident = "seurat_annotations",              plot_type = "circos", labels_rot = TRUE)   # Pie plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "pie")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "pie", split_by = "stim")   # Ring plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "ring", group_by = "stim",              palette = "Spectral") #> 'frac' is forced to 'group' for 'ring' plot.   # Trend plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "trend", frac = "group",              x_text_angle = 90, group_by = c("stim", "seurat_annotations")) #> Multiple columns are provided in 'group_by'. They will be concatenated into one column.   # Sankey plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "sankey", group_by = c("seurat_clusters", "stim"),              links_alpha = .6) #> Missing alluvia for some stratum combinations.  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "sankey", links_alpha = .6,              group_by = c("stim", "seurat_annotations", "orig.ident")) #> Missing alluvia for some stratum combinations.  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "sankey", links_alpha = .6,              group_by = c("seurat_clusters", "stim", "seurat_annotations", "orig.ident")) #> Missing alluvia for some stratum combinations.   # Area plot CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "area", frac = "group", x_text_angle = 90,              group_by = "seurat_annotations", split_by = "stim")   # Heatmap CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "heatmap", group_by = "stim", palette = "Blues")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "heatmap", group_by = "stim",    frac = "group", columns_split_by = "seurat_annotations", swap = TRUE)   # Pies # Simulate some sets of cells (e.g. clones) ifnb_sub$r1 <- ifelse(ifnb_sub$seurat_clusters %in% c("0", "1", "2"), 1, 0) ifnb_sub$r2 <- sample(c(1, 0), ncol(ifnb_sub), prob = c(0.5, 0.5), replace = TRUE) ifnb_sub$r3 <- sample(c(1, 0), ncol(ifnb_sub), prob = c(0.7, 0.3), replace = TRUE) CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "pies", group_by = "stim", rows_name = "Clones",    rows_by = c("r1", "r2", "r3"), column_names_side = "top", cluster_columns = FALSE,    row_names_side = "right", pie_size = "identity", pie_values = "sum")   # Expand pies into heatmap CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "heatmap", group_by = "stim", rows_name = "Clones",    rows_by = c("r1", "r2", "r3"), column_names_side = "top", cluster_columns = FALSE,    row_names_side = "right")   # Rows split by clones instead of idents and show fraction of cells in each clone. CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "heatmap", group_by = "stim", frac = "group",   rows_split_by = c("r1", "r2", "r3"), column_names_side = "top", cluster_columns = FALSE,   row_names_side = "right", label = function(x) scales::number(x, accuracy = 0.01),   cell_type = "label")   # Radar plot/Spider plot pr <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "radar", group_by = "stim") ps <- CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, plot_type = "spider", group_by = "stim") pr | ps   # Box/Violin plot ifnb_sub$group <- sample(paste0("g", 1:10), nrow(ifnb_sub), replace = TRUE) CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = c("group", "stim"), frac = "group",    plot_type = "violin", add_box = TRUE, ident = "seurat_annotations",    x_text_angle = 60, comparisons = TRUE, aspect.ratio = 0.8) #> Warning: [Box/Violin/BeeswarmPlot] Some pairwise comparisons may fail due to insufficient data points or variability. Adjusting data to ensure valid comparisons.   # Use different agg other than counting the number of cells. # Let's say we do the fraction of g1 in each stim group. CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, agg = "sum(group == 'g1') / n()",    plot_type = "bar", ylab = "Fraction of g1 cells")  CellStatPlot(ifnb_sub, group_by = "stim", agg = "sum(group == 'g1') / n()",    plot_type = "bar", ylab = "Fraction of g1 cells")  # }

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: Visualizes RNA velocity reduced-dimension embedding. RNA velocity infers future transcriptional state individual cells modeling ratio unspliced (nascent) spliced (mature) mRNA transcripts. dimension reduction plot, velocity displayed arrows (grid/stream fields) showing predicted direction magnitude transcriptional change cell — effectively revealing "flow" cells differentiation, development, state transitions. CellVelocityPlot serves unified interface across multiple single-cell data containers: Seurat objects — Extracts embeddings main reduction (reduction) velocity reduction (v_reduction) via Embeddings(); metadata grouping via @meta.data. Giotto objects — Extracts dimension reductions via getDimReduction() using spat_unit feat_type identify correct spatial unit feature type. h5ad files (.h5ad opened H5File) — Reads obsm main velocity embeddings; obs metadata. Reduction names automatically prefixed "X_" needed.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
code: CellVelocityPlot(   object,   reduction,   v_reduction,   spat_unit = NULL,   feat_type = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   ident = NULL,   ... )

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: object Seurat object, Giotto object, path .h5ad file, opened H5File hdf5r package. reduction Name main dimension reduction defines cell positions plot (e.g., "umap", "pca", "tsne"). reduction must already exist object. Unlike CellDimPlot, parameter required — default. v_reduction Name velocity reduction encodes RNA velocity vectors (e.g., "stochastic_UMAP", "dynamical_PCA"). first two dimensions used. reduction must already exist object typically generated RNA velocity analysis tools velocyto.R scVelo (Python, exported h5ad). spat_unit Spatial unit name Giotto objects (e.g., "cell"). Ignored Seurat h5ad inputs. NULL, default spatial unit auto-detected via GiottoClass::set_default_spat_unit(). feat_type Feature type name Giotto objects (e.g., "rna", "dna", "protein"). Ignored Seurat h5ad inputs. NULL, default feature type auto-detected via GiottoClass::set_default_feat_type(). group_by Metadata column name used color cells group (e.g., "CellType", "Phase"). Unlike CellDimPlot, parameter optional — NULL, cells plotted uniform color, can useful focusing velocity flow patterns without visual distraction cluster colors. ident Alias group_by, provided compatibility Seurat's naming convention. group_by ident specified, must identical. ... Additional arguments passed plotthis::VelocityPlot(). Key parameters include: Plot type: plot_type — "arrow" (default), "grid", "stream" (see Velocity plot types section) Appearance: pt_size, pt_alpha, arrow_length, arrow_thickness, palette, palcolor, theme, theme_args, legend.position Grid parameters (plot_type = "grid"): grid_n, grid_color, grid_alpha, grid_lwd Stream parameters (plot_type = "stream"): stream_n, stream_color, stream_alpha, stream_lwd Layout: split_by, combine, nrow, ncol

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: ggplot object.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: See: https://pwwang.github.io/scplotter/articles/Working_with_anndata_h5ad_files.html examples using function .h5ad files.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: Velocity computation prerequisites: function visualizes pre-computed RNA velocity. Velocity must calculated separately using tools velocyto.R (R) scVelo (Python). computed velocity reduction must already exist object calling CellVelocityPlot. Velocity overlay CellDimPlot: quick velocity overlay standard dimension reduction plot (DimPlot features like highlighting, density contours, group marks), use CellDimPlot(velocity = "stochastic_UMAP") instead. CellVelocityPlot best need grid stream visualizations specifically. Required parameters: reduction v_reduction required defaults — unlike CellDimPlot() reduction can auto-detected Seurat objects. velocity visualization intrinsically requires two distinct reductions, auto-detection velocity reduction reliable across analysis pipelines.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: RNA velocity requires two dimension reductions stored object: Main reduction (reduction): standard dimension reduction (e.g., UMAP, PCA, t-SNE) computed cells' current expression profiles. defines positions cells plot. Velocity reduction (v_reduction): dimension reduction computed velocity vectors derived spliced/unspliced RNA ratios. first two dimensions reduction encode direction magnitude predicted transcriptional change. Velocity reductions typically follow naming convention "<model>_<reduction>", <model> one : "stochastic" — Stochastic model transcriptional dynamics "deterministic" — Deterministic model assuming constant transcription rates "dynamical" — Dynamical model accounting time-dependent transcription rates example, "stochastic_UMAP" "dynamical_PCA".

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: CellDimPlot can overlay velocity arrows dimension reduction plot via velocity parameter, CellVelocityPlot dedicated velocity visualization function. key differences : CellVelocityPlot delegates plotthis::VelocityPlot(), supports multiple plot types: "arrow" (default), "grid", "stream". CellVelocityPlot requires reduction v_reduction explicit arguments (defaults). CellVelocityPlot support full set DimPlot overlays (marks, density, lineages, etc.) — focuses solely velocity visualization.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
text: Passed via ... plotthis's VelocityPlot(), plot_type parameter controls velocities rendered: "arrow" (default) — arrow drawn cell's current position predicted future position. Best small medium datasets individual cell trajectories interest. "grid" — embedding space divided grid, average velocity vectors computed per grid cell. Reduces visual clutter large datasets shows regional trends. "stream" — Streamlines computed velocity field, showing continuous flow trajectories. Best visualizing differentiation paths developmental trajectories.

title: Cell Velocity Plot — CellVelocityPlot
code: # \donttest{ data(pancreas_sub)  # Arrow plot (default) — each cell shows its predicted direction CellVelocityPlot(pancreas_sub, reduction = "PCA", v_reduction = "stochastic_PCA")   # Grid plot — velocity vectors averaged over grid cells CellVelocityPlot(pancreas_sub, reduction = "PCA", v_reduction = "stochastic_PCA",   plot_type = "grid")   # Stream plot — continuous flow trajectories CellVelocityPlot(pancreas_sub, reduction = "PCA", v_reduction = "stochastic_PCA",   plot_type = "stream")   # With group coloring CellVelocityPlot(pancreas_sub, reduction = "PCA", v_reduction = "stochastic_PCA",   group_by = "SubCellType")  # }

title: Clonal Abundance Plot — ClonalAbundancePlot
text: Visualizes distribution clonal abundances — many clones present abundance level (frequency) repertoire. Clonal abundance distributions typically follow power-law pattern: small number highly expanded clones large number rare clones. function helps characterize repertoire structure showing whether immune response dominated large clones (clonal expansion) evenly distributed across many clones (high diversity). ClonalAbundancePlot computes clonal abundance data via scRepertoire::clonalAbundance() visualizes trend lines, histograms, density curves.

title: Clonal Abundance Plot — ClonalAbundancePlot

title: Clonal Composition Plot — ClonalCompositionPlot
text: Visualizes composition immune repertoire categorizing clones abundance groups (Rare, Small, Medium, Large, Hyperexpanded) plotting relative proportions across samples metadata groups. reveals overall structure repertoire — whether dominated large clones (clonal expansion) composed many small clones (high diversity). ClonalCompositionPlot supports three analysis methods: Homeostasis ("homeostasis", "homeo", "rel") — Clones binned frequency (fraction total repertoire) categories Rare, Small, Medium, Large, Hyperexpanded. Uses scRepertoire::clonalHomeostasis(). Top clones ("top") — Clones ranked binned rank index (e.g., top 10, top 100, etc.). Uses scRepertoire::clonalProportion(). Rare clones ("rare") — Clones binned absolute size (clone count). Uses clone size thresholds directly.

title: Clonal Composition Plot — ClonalCompositionPlot
code: ClonalCompositionPlot(   data,   clone_call = "aa",   chain = "both",   method = c("homeostasis", "homeo", "rel", "top", "rare"),   clone_split = NULL,   scale = TRUE,   facet_by = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   plot_type = c("bar", "ring", "box", "violin"),   order = NULL,   ... )

One : "homeostasis" (default), "homeo", "rel" — Frequency-based binning using clone_split abundance thresholds. "top" — Rank-based binning using clone_split rank cutoffs. "rare" — Size-based binning using clone_split clone size thresholds. clone_split Threshold values defining clonal categories. Default NULL, picks sensible defaults per method: "homeostasis"/"homeo"/"rel" — named list frequency thresholds: list(Rare = 1e-04, Small = 0.001, Medium = 0.01, Large = 0.1, Hyperexpanded = 1) "top" — rank cutoffs: c(10, 100, 1000, 10000, 30000, 100000) "rare" — clone size thresholds: c(1, 3, 10, 30, 100) scale normalize values. One : TRUE (default) — Values within x-axis group sum 1 (group-wise proportion). FALSE — Raw values (clone counts) used. Default NULL. Required "box" "violin" plot types. split_by Metadata column used split data separate plots. Default NULL. xlab X-axis label. One : "bar" (default) — Stacked bar chart clonal categories across groups. Best comparing composition across categories. "ring" — Ring (donut) chart alternative stacked bars. "box" — Box plot showing distribution clonal category's abundance across samples. Requires group_by. "violin" — Violin plot alternative box plot. Requires group_by. order named list controlling order factor levels. List names column names; list values desired order. Default NULL. ... title: Clonal Diversity Plot — ClonalDiversityPlot
text: Visualizes clonal diversity metrics across samples metadata groups. Clonal diversity quantifies richness evenness immune repertoire — many distinct clonotypes present evenly cells distributed among . High diversity indicates broad, well-distributed repertoire; low diversity may indicate clonal expansion (oligoclonality) response antigen stimulation disease. ClonalDiversityPlot computes diversity scores using custom implementation wraps several scRepertoire methods adds three scplotter-specific metrics (Gini coefficient, D50, DXX). Results visualized bar, box, violin plots.

title: Clonal Diversity Plot — ClonalDiversityPlot
text: Bootstrap support: underlying ClonalDiversity() function supports bootstrap resampling (n_boots). exposed ClonalDiversityPlot directly used internally. group_by required box/violin: group_by parameter required plot_type "box" "violin". types show per-sample distributions grouped group_by variable.

title: Clonal Diversity Plot — ClonalDiversityPlot
text: method parameter selects diversity metric: Richness evenness metrics: "shannon" (default) — Shannon entropy index. Higher values indicate greater diversity. Sensitive richness evenness. "inv.simpson" — Inverse Simpson index. effective number equally abundant clones. Less sensitive rare clones Shannon. "norm.entropy" — Normalized entropy (Pielou's evenness). Shannon entropy divided log richness; ranges 0 1. "gini.simpson" — Gini-Simpson index. probability two randomly selected cells belong different clones. Richness estimators (account unobserved clones): "chao1" — Chao1 richness estimator. Estimates total number clones including yet observed, based number singletons doubletons. "ACE" — Abundance-based Coverage Estimator. Estimates richness correction sample coverage. scplotter-specific metrics: "gini.coeff" — Gini coefficient. Measures inequality clone size distribution. 0 indicates perfect equality (clones size); 1 indicates perfect inequality (one clone dominates). "d50" — number top clones together account 50% total repertoire. "dXX" — number top clones together account XX% total repertoire. Use d parameter set percentage.

title: Clonal Diversity Plot — ClonalDiversityPlot
code: set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,     samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L")) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Type",     variables = factor(rep(c("B", "L"), 4), levels = c("L", "B")) ) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Subject",     variables = rep(c("P17", "P18", "P19", "P20"), each = 2) )  ClonalDiversityPlot(data)  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type")  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "box")  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "violin")  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "violin",   method = "gini.coeff", add_box = TRUE)  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "violin",   method = "inv.simpson", add_box = TRUE)  ClonalDiversityPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "violin",   method = "d50", add_box = TRUE)

what: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: Adaptive immune receptors (TCRs BCRs) assembled V(D)J recombination, variable (V), diversity (D), joining (J) gene segments randomly selected rearranged. frequency different gene segments used — termed gene usage — provides insight immune repertoire composition, T/B cell development, antigen-driven selection. Skewed gene usage can indicate clonal expansion, immune aging, disease-associated repertoire bias.

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
code: ClonalGeneUsagePlot(   data,   genes = "TRBV",   scale = TRUE,   top = 20,   order = NULL,   plot_type = c("bar", "heatmap", "circos", "chord", "alluvial", "sankey"),   group_by = "Sample",   facet_by = NULL,   facet_ncol = 1,   split_by = NULL,   aspect.ratio = 2/top,   theme_args = list(),   ylab = NULL,   show_row_names = TRUE,   show_column_names = TRUE,   row_annotation = NULL,   row_annotation_type = list(),   row_annotation_side = "right",   row_annotation_agg = list(),   ... )

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot

Must contain columns gene segment information (e.g., TRBV_, TRBJ_ TCR beta chain). genes character vector gene segment prefixes analyze. Default "TRBV". single prefix provided (e.g., "TRBV"), second dimension plot group_by variable. two prefixes provided (e.g., c("TRBV", "TRBJ")), axes represent gene segments, revealing V-J pairing frequencies, group_by used faceting. Must length 1 2. scale Logical; whether normalize gene usage proportions within group (TRUE, default), display raw counts (FALSE). Setting scale = TRUE recommended comparing samples different sequencing depths, accounts differences total clone counts. Raw counts may preferred absolute clone numbers biologically meaningful. top Integer; number top genes (single gene mode) top gene pairs (dual gene mode) display. Genes ranked total usage across groups. Default 20. Increase value show genes, decrease focus dominant segments. order named list specifying order factor levels grouping variables. example, list(Sample = c("P17B", "P17L")) list(Type = c("L", "B")). Default NULL, uses order present data. Names list must match column names data. plot_type Character; type visualization. One "bar" (default), "heatmap", "circos", "chord", "alluvial", "sankey". Note "alluvial" "sankey" treated identically. See Plot types section guidance selecting appropriate visualization. group_by Character vector; column(s) data group . Default "Sample". single gene analysis, becomes second dimension plot. dual gene analysis, used faceting. Set NULL disable grouping. facet_by character vector column names facet plots . Default NULL. parameter typically set automatically function specified manually — providing value raise error. facet_ncol Integer; number columns facet grid faceting applied. Default 1. relevant plot types produce faceted output (e.g., sankey dual gene mode). split_by character vector column names split plots . Default NULL. provided, separate plots generated combination values specified columns. dual gene analysis, split_by used single gene mode; dual gene mode, plot automatically split group_by specifying split_by raise error. aspect.ratio Numeric; aspect ratio (height / width) bar plot panels. Default 2 / top, automatically scales ratio based number genes displayed. applicable plot_type = "bar". theme_args named list arguments passed ggplot2::theme() customizing plot appearance. bar plots, panel.grid.major.y defaults element_blank() cleaner look. ylab Character; y-axis label. Default NULL, automatically uses "Gene Usage Fraction" scale = TRUE "Gene Usage Count" scale = FALSE. show_row_names Logical; whether display row names (gene segment names) heatmap. Default TRUE. Set FALSE hide gene labels many display legibly. applicable plot_type = "heatmap". show_column_names Logical; whether display column names (sample/ group names second gene set names) heatmap. Default TRUE. applicable plot_type = "heatmap". row_annotation named list specifying row annotations heatmap plots. element column name data use annotation data. title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: ClonalGeneUsagePlot visualizes usage frequency TCR BCR gene segments across samples conditions. supports two modes analysis: Single gene analysis (genes = "TRBV"): second axis group_by variable (typically Sample), producing gene--sample matrix suitable comparing usage across conditions. Dual gene analysis (genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ")): axes gene segments, group_by variable used faceting. reveals preferential V-J pairings, can reflect structural constraints receptor antigen-driven selection specific chain combinations. Gene usage can displayed raw counts proportions scaled within group (scale = TRUE), latter appropriate comparing samples different sequencing depths. function uses scRepertoire::vizGenes() internally compute gene usage frequencies, passes result appropriate plotthis visualization function.

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: Gene usage data computed using scRepertoire::vizGenes(), requires input data contain gene segment columns (e.g., TRBV_gene, TRBJ_gene). columns automatically created scRepertoire::combineTCR() scRepertoire::combineBCR(). bar plots, aspect.ratio automatically set 2 / top accommodate varying numbers genes. can override passing custom value. facet_by parameter set internally function provided user. raise error. Dual gene analysis split_by chord/circos mode supported — plot automatically split group_by instead. using scale = TRUE, proportions sum 1 within group gene set, making possible compare relative usage across groups different sizes. However, normalization can mask differences absolute clone counts — use scale = FALSE total clone numbers interest. Gene segment names follow IMGT nomenclature. Ensure gene prefixes match naming convention dataset (e.g., "TRBV" human TRB V genes, "Trbv" mouse).

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: genes parameter determines dimensionality analysis: Single gene prefix (e.g., "TRBV", "TRBJ", "IGHV"): x-axis shows gene segments (e.g., TRBV5-1, TRBV7-2), y-axis (columns heatmap) shows group_by variable, typically Sample. standard mode comparing gene usage across samples conditions. Use bar plot quickly identify - -represented genes, heatmap compact overview many genes across many samples. Two gene prefixes (e.g., c("TRBV", "TRBJ")): x-axis shows first gene set y-axis (columns) shows second gene set. cell represents specific V-J pair, group_by variable used faceting. mode essential studying chain pairing — certain V segments preferentially pair specific J segments, deviations expected pairing frequencies can indicate structural constraints disease-associated repertoire features. sankey/alluvial plot type particularly effective dual gene analysis, showing flow one gene set .

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: Five visualization types available, suited different analytical goals: "bar" (default): Horizontal bar chart showing gene usage per sample/group. Best comparing moderate number genes across samples. Gene labels rotated 90 degrees readability. aspect.ratio parameter controls bar height (defaults 2 / top automatically scale number genes). "heatmap": Matrix heatmap genes rows samples/conditions columns. Ideal surveying many genes across many samples simultaneously. single gene prefix used, "Total Usage" row annotation automatically added showing aggregate usage per gene line plot. Supports custom row annotations, annotation types, aggregation functions. "circos" / "chord": Chord diagram showing flow gene segments samples (single gene) one gene set another (dual gene). Chord diagrams excel revealing broad patterns connectivity. dual gene mode, plot automatically split group_by variable. "alluvial" / "sankey": Sankey diagram showing flow gene segments samples (single gene) one gene set another (dual gene). Unlike chord diagrams, sankey diagrams preserve ordering categories often easier read many connections. dual gene mode, plot faceted group_by. Note "alluvial" automatically mapped "sankey".

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
text: Gene segment prefixes follow standard IMGT nomenclature: Human TCR: TRBV, TRBD, TRBJ (beta chain); TRAV, TRAJ (alpha chain); TRGV, TRGJ (gamma chain); TRDV, TRDJ (delta chain) Human BCR: IGHV, IGHD, IGHJ (heavy chain); IGKV, IGKJ (kappa chain); IGLV, IGLJ (lambda chain) Mouse: Prefixes similar use Trbv, Ighv, etc. (sentence case) gene prefix used identify matching columns data. genes matching prefix included analysis. top parameter selects frequently used genes/genepairs display.

title: Visualize TCR/BCR gene segment usage — ClonalGeneUsagePlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,     samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L")) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Type",     variables = factor(rep(c("B", "L"), 4), levels = c("L", "B")) ) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Subject",     variables = rep(c("P17", "P18", "P19", "P20"), each = 2) )  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data)  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = c("TRBJ", "TRBV")) #> Warning: Content-based width (6.9) conflicts with aspect.ratio (0.1). Using content width; plot panel aspect ratio will differ from specified.  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, top = 40, plot_type = "heatmap")  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"), plot_type = "heatmap")  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = "TRBV", group_by = "Type", plot_type = "chord")  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"), group_by = "Type", plot_type = "chord")  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"), plot_type = "alluvial",      facet_scales = "free_y") #> Missing alluvia for some stratum combinations.  ClonalGeneUsagePlot(data, genes = c("TRBV", "TRBJ"), plot_type = "alluvial",      group_by = NULL) #> Missing alluvia for some stratum combinations.  # }

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: Short amino acid motifs within CDR3 sequences — termed k-mers — can reveal shared binding specificities, common structural elements, repertoire-level sequence features apparent full-length sequence analysis alone. Specific k-mers may enriched responses particular antigens, represent public TCR/BCR motifs shared across individuals, reflect convergent recombination events.

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: ggplot object (list ggplot objects combine = FALSE passed via ...)

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: ClonalKmerPlot extracts k-mers length k CDR3 amino acid (nucleotide) sequences visualizes frequency across samples conditions. analysis uses scRepertoire::percentKmer() identify top frequent k-mers, displays using bar, line, heatmap visualizations. K-mer analysis complementary positional analysis (ClonalPositionalPlot): positional analysis asks "happens position X?", k-mer analysis asks "short sequence motifs appear, regardless exact position?" position-independent perspective can capture sequence features distributed across different CDR3 locations.

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: K-mer extraction performed scRepertoire::percentKmer(), uses sliding window across CDR3 sequences. resulting frequencies represent percentage sequences containing k-mer least . bar plots, facet_by internally set group_by must provided user. number possible k-mers grows exponentially k (20^k amino acids), top frequent displayed. Ensure top set high enough capture motifs interest. K-mer frequencies can noisy sample sizes small. Consider using split_by facet_by disaggregate data rather relying small within-group sample sizes. nucleotide k-mers (clone_call = "nt"), alphabet size 4 rather 20, shorter k values (2-3) generally appropriate.

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: choice k represents fundamental trade-: k = 2 (dipeptides): Highly recurrent often non-specific — many dipeptides appear functionally unrelated receptors. Best broad surveys amino acid pairing preferences. k = 3 (tripeptides, default): commonly used length. Tripeptides long enough capture meaningful motifs (e.g., "CASS" motif start TRB CDR3s) short enough recur frequently across samples robust statistical analysis. k = 4 5: specific motifs likely reflect genuine functional constraints antigen-driven selection. However, longer k-mers appear less frequently, requiring larger datasets reliable frequency estimation. top parameter controls many frequent k-mers displayed. default 25 provides manageable view bar line plots; increase heatmap visualizations can accommodate many motifs.

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
text: Three visualization types available: "bar" (default): Bar chart k-mer frequencies, faceted group_by. Best comparing motif usage across moderate number samples conditions. facet_by available (set internally). "line": Line plot connecting k-mer frequencies, group separate line. Useful visualizing trends across motifs comparing frequency profile shape conditions. Supports facet_by additional faceting dimensions. "heatmap": K-mer group matrix showing frequency color intensity. Ideal surveying many motifs across many samples simultaneously, revealing clusters co-enriched motifs.

title: Visualize CDR3 k-mer (motif) frequency — ClonalKmerPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,     samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L")) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Type",     variables = factor(rep(c("B", "L"), 4), levels = c("L", "B")) ) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Subject",     variables = rep(c("P17", "P18", "P19", "P20"), each = 2) )  ClonalKmerPlot(data)  ClonalKmerPlot(data, group_by = "Type")  ClonalKmerPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "line")  ClonalKmerPlot(data, group_by = "Type", plot_type = "heatmap")  # }

title: Clonal CDR3 Length Plot — ClonalLengthPlot
text: Visualizes distribution CDR3 sequence lengths across immune repertoire. CDR3 length key feature T-cell B-cell receptor diversity — different clones different CDR3 lengths, shifts length distribution can indicate clonal selection, antigen-specific expansion, repertoire bias. ClonalLengthPlot computes CDR3 length data via scRepertoire::clonalLength() visualizes distribution bar, box, violin, density plots. Length measured amino acids (clone_call = "aa") nucleotides (clone_call = "nt").

title: Clonal Overlap Plot — ClonalOverlapPlot
text: Visualizes overlap (sharing) T-cell B-cell clonotypes samples metadata groups heatmap. cell heatmap quantifies degree clonal sharing two groups, using one several similarity overlap metrics. key analysis identifying public clones shared across individuals, tracking antigen-specific clones across time points tissues, comparing repertoire similarity conditions. ClonalOverlapPlot computes pairwise overlap via scRepertoire::clonalOverlap() visualizes resulting matrix labeled heatmap using plotthis::Heatmap().

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: complementarity-determining region 3 (CDR3) variable region T cell B cell receptors, primary determinant antigen specificity. Analyzing amino acid composition, diversity, physicochemical properties vary across CDR3 positions provides insight repertoire structure, selection pressures, biophysical constraints shape antigen recognition.

Common values include "TRA" (TCR alpha), "TRB" (TCR beta), "TRG" (TCR gamma), "TRD" (TCR delta), "IGH" (BCR heavy), "IGL" (BCR lambda), "IGK" (BCR kappa). chain determines CDR3 column used analysis. aa_length Integer; number CDR3 amino acid positions analyze, counting N-terminus. "norm.entropy" — Normalized entropy (diversity, scaled range 0–1). "Atchley" — Atchley factor scores (5 properties). "Kidera" — Kidera factor scores (10 properties). Default NULL. Note AA property bar plots, facet_by set internally specified manually (raise error). entropy line plots, can used add additional faceting dimension. facet_ncol Integer; number columns facet grid. Default NULL. property bar plots, defaults number properties selected factor set. aspect.ratio Numeric; aspect ratio (height / width) plot panels. Default NULL, uses method- plot_type-appropriate defaults (e.g., 2 / aa_length entropy bar plots, 6 / aa_length entropy line plots, 10 / aa_length box/violin plots). ... title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: ggplot object (list ggplot objects combine = FALSE passed via ...)

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: ClonalPositionalPlot computes visualizes position-level properties CDR3 sequences aligning N-terminus (Cys residue) analyzing position independently. Three fundamentally different categories analysis available method parameter: Amino acid frequency (method = "AA"): Raw frequency amino acid position, displayed stacked bars pie-chart heatmap. reveals position-specific amino acid preferences (e.g., hydrophobic residues center CDR3 loop, glycine enrichment turn positions). Positional entropy (method = "shannon", "inv.simpson", "norm.entropy"): Quantifies diversity amino acid usage position. Low entropy position indicates conservation (often structural constraint), high entropy indicates variability (potential antigen contact). Supports bar, line, heatmap, box, violin plot types, box/violin requiring group_by variable comparisons. Physicochemical properties (method = "Atchley", "Kidera", "stScales", "tScales", "VHSE"): amino acid represented set numeric factor scores capture physicochemical properties (e.g., hydrophobicity, size, charge, secondary structure propensity). averaged position reveal trends along CDR3 sequence. Supports bar line plot types. function delegates computation three scRepertoire functions: scRepertoire::percentAA() amino acid frequency, scRepertoire::positionalEntropy() entropy metrics, scRepertoire::positionalProperty() physicochemical property scores.

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: Positional analysis requires CDR3 amino acid sequences. automatically extracted scRepertoire data processing. Ensure input data processed scRepertoire::combineTCR() scRepertoire::combineBCR(). "AA" method, facet_by internally set group_by must provided user. applies property bar plots. Box violin plot types (entropy methods ) require group_by explicitly set value default "Sample". plots need meaningful groups compare distributions. Property methods ("Atchley", "Kidera", etc.) support "bar", "line", cases "heatmap" plot types. Box violin plots available property methods. CDR3 sequences shorter aa_length missing data C-terminal positions. can introduce noise positions, especially entropy property analyses. Consider length distribution CDR3s choosing aa_length. "AA" heatmap uses pie-chart cell type (cell_type = "pie"), cell represents position pie segments show amino acid composition. distinctive visualization unique method.

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: method parameter selects computational method determines plot types available: Amino acid frequency ("AA"): Computes frequency 20 standard amino acids every CDR3 position aa_length. Plot types: "bar" — Stacked bar chart one bar per position, colored amino acid. Faceted group_by. facet_by available (set internally). "heatmap" — Pie-chart heatmap cell position pie segments show amino acid proportions position. Groups rows. Entropy methods ("shannon", "inv.simpson", "norm.entropy"): produces single diversity value per position per sample. Plot types: "bar" — Bar chart diversity per position, faceted group_by. "line" — Line plot connecting positions, group separate line. Useful comparing diversity trends conditions. "heatmap" — Position--group matrix diversity values. "box" — Box plot summarizing diversity across positions group. Requires group_by provided ("Sample"). "violin" — Violin plot variant box plot. Requires group_by. Property methods ("Atchley", "Kidera", "stScales", "tScales", "VHSE"): yields multiple numeric property scores per position. Plot types: "bar" — Mean property values per position, faceted property group_by. facet_by available (set internally). "line" — Line plot mean property values position, group_by line grouping "property" faceting.

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: property method represents amino acids using different set derived factors. choice depends biological question: Atchley factors ("Atchley"): Five factors derived 494 amino acid properties — Factor (polarity / hydrophobicity), Factor II (secondary structure), Factor III (molecular size), Factor IV (codon diversity), Factor V (electrostatic charge). Widely used interpretable. Kidera factors ("Kidera"): Ten factors derived 188 physical properties amino acids, capturing granular property variation Atchley factors. VHSE ("VHSE"): Vectors Hydrophobic, Steric, Electronic properties — eight descriptors derived 50 physicochemical properties. stScales ("stScales"): set scales derived structural topology proteins (ST-scale), emphasizing local structural context. tScales ("tScales"): T-scales derived topological properties amino acids (T-scale), focusing structural folding properties.

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
text: aa_length parameter controls many positions CDR3 analyzed. CDR3 sequences aligned N-terminus (conserved cysteine residue), positions beyond aa_length excluded. Sequences shorter given position contribute data position. means: larger aa_length includes positions C-terminal positions may sparser data (fewer sequences reach lengths). default 20 amino acids captures majority typical TCR beta chain CDR3 lengths, though BCR heavy chain CDR3s can substantially longer. entropy property methods, sparse positions may show artificially low noisy values — consider distribution CDR3 lengths dataset setting aa_length.

title: Visualize positional properties of CDR3 sequences — ClonalPositionalPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,    samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L")) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,   variable.name = "Type",   variables = factor(rep(c("B", "L"), 4), levels = c("L", "B")) )  ClonalPositionalPlot(data)  ClonalPositionalPlot(data, method = "shannon")  ClonalPositionalPlot(data, method = "norm.entropy", plot_type = "heatmap")  ClonalPositionalPlot(data, method = "Atchley", group_by = "Type", plot_type = "bar")  ClonalPositionalPlot(data, method = "Atchley", plot_type = "line")  # }

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
text: Visualizes clonal rarefaction curves — estimates clone richness function sampling depth. Rarefaction addresses fundamental challenge immune repertoire analysis: number clones observed depends many cells sequenced. repeatedly subsampling (bootstrapping) data varying depths, rarefaction curves reveal whether repertoire sampled saturation whether additional sequencing uncover many clones. ClonalRarefactionPlot extracts clone count data repertoire, optionally groups metadata columns, generates rarefaction curves via plotthis::RarefactionPlot(). split_by specified, separate plots generated split group combined multi-panel layout.

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
code: ClonalRarefactionPlot(   data,   clone_call = "aa",   chain = "both",   group_by = "Sample",   group_by_sep = "_",   order = NULL,   n_boots = 20,   q = 0,   facet_by = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   palette = "Paired",   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   ... )

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
text: data product scRepertoire::combineTCR(), scRepertoire::combineBCR(), scRepertoire::combineExpression(). clone_call define clone. One "gene", "nt", "aa" (default), "strict", custom variable name data. chain chain(s) use: "" (default), "TRA", "TRB", "TRD", "TRG", "IGH", "IGL". group_by Metadata column(s) used define curves (unique group produces one rarefaction curve). Multiple columns concatenated using group_by_sep. Default "Sample". group_by_sep Separator used concatenating multiple group_by columns. Default "_". order named list controlling order factor levels. List names column names; list values desired order. Default NULL. n_boots Number bootstrap iterations estimating confidence intervals. Higher values produce smoother confidence bands increase computation time. Default 20. q diversity order (Hill number). 0 species richness, 1 Shannon entropy, 2 Simpson index. Default 0. See Hill numbers section details. facet_by supported ClonalRarefactionPlot. Use split_by group_by instead. Must NULL. split_by Metadata column used split data separate rarefaction plots. specified, independent rarefaction performed split group, plots combined. Default NULL. split_by_sep Separator used concatenating multiple split_by columns. Default "_". palette Color palette distinguishing curves different groups. Default "Paired". combine Logical; TRUE (default), multiple plots (split_by) combined single layout. nrow Number rows combined plot layout. Default NULL (auto-determined). ncol Number columns combined plot layout. Default NULL (auto-determined). byrow Logical; TRUE (default), combined layout filled row row. ... Additional arguments passed plotthis::RarefactionPlot(). Key parameters include: type — Plot type: 1 (line ), 2 (line confidence band), 3 (confidence band ). title — Plot title. xlab, ylab — Axis labels.

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
text: ggplot object, list ggplot objects combine = FALSE.

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
text: Bootstrap iterations: n_boots parameter controls number resampling iterations. Higher values give stable estimates increase computation time linearly. exploratory analysis, n_boots = 20 typically sufficient; publication-quality figures, consider using n_boots = 100 . facet_by supported: Unlike many scplotter functions, ClonalRarefactionPlot support facet_by. Use split_by separate plots group_by show multiple curves axes.

title: Clonal Rarefaction Plot — ClonalRarefactionPlot
text: q parameter selects diversity order (Hill number) used rarefaction: q = 0 — Species richness (clone count). Counts number distinct clonotypes regardless size. sensitive rare clones. q = 1 — Shannon entropy (exponential). Weighs clones proportionally abundance. Balances rare dominant clones. q = 2 — Simpson index (inverse). Weighs dominant clones heavily. Least sensitive rare clones. Higher values q increasingly emphasize abundant clones rare ones.

Consider specifying shapes manually if you need #>   that many of them. #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). title: Clonal Residency Plot — ClonalResidencyPlot
text: Visualizes sharing (residency) T-cell B-cell clones across different samples metadata groups. Clonal residency analysis reveals clonotypes distributed — whether clone private one condition shared across multiple conditions — critical understanding immune responses, tracking antigen-specific clones, identifying public vs. private repertoires. ClonalResidencyPlot supports three visualization modes: Scatter plot — Compares clone sizes two groups log-transformed axes. Points colored clonal category: singletons (unique one group), expanded clones, dual clones (shared groups). Correlation statistics displayed subtitle. Venn diagram — Shows overlap clone sets 4 groups. with_class = TRUE, labels include singlet counts. UpSet plot — Shows intersection sizes number groups. with_class = TRUE, clone classes (singlet, expanded) displayed separate intersections.

title: Clonal Residency Plot — ClonalResidencyPlot

what: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: ClonalStatPlot provides unified interface visualizing abundance, frequency, dynamics T cell B cell clones across experimental groups. versatile clone visualization function scplotter, offering multiple plot types different analytical purposes. function operates output scRepertoire::combineTCR(), scRepertoire::combineBCR(), scRepertoire::combineExpression(). Clones identified CDR3 amino acid sequence, nucleotide sequence, V(D)J gene usage, combination thereof (via clone_call). function computes clone-level statistics (size, fraction, count clones) within group renders using one ten supported plot types. defining feature ClonalStatPlot flexible clone selection system. Clones can specified directly IDs, selected programmatically using expression selectors top(), sel(), shared(), uniq(), comparison operators (gt(), lt(), eq(), etc.). selectors evaluate within context faceting/splitting group, enabling per-group selection expanded clones, clones shared conditions, clones meeting custom abundance thresholds. See Clone selection section CloneSelectors full details. Clones can also aggregated named groups (passing named list clones), group defined selection expression. mode, visualization unit becomes clone group rather individual clones, enabling comparisons "hyper-expanded clones condition " vs. "hyper-expanded clones condition B."

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
code: ClonalStatPlot(   data,   clones = "top(10)",   clone_call = "aa",   chain = "both",   values_by = c("count", "fraction", "n"),   plot_type = c("bar", "box", "violin", "heatmap", "pies", "circos", "chord", "sankey",     "alluvial", "trend", "col"),   group_by = "Sample",   groups = NULL,   subgroup_by = NULL,   subgroups = NULL,   order = NULL,   within_subgroup = match.arg(plot_type) != "pies",   relabel = plot_type %in% c("col", "chord", "circos"),   facet_by = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   y = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   ... )

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: data product scRepertoire::combineTCR(), scRepertoire::combineBCR(), scRepertoire::combineExpression(). list data frames element represents sample, columns clone identifiers (CTaa, CTnt, CTgene, etc.) cell-level metadata. clones clones track visualize. Default: "top(10)". Accepts three forms: (1) character vector clone IDs, (2) single selection expression string (e.g. "top(10)", "sel(P17B > 5, group_by = 'Sample')"), (3) named list selection expressions define clone groups (e.g. list(Expanded = "sel(> 20, group_by = 'Sample')")). See Clone selection section details. single unnamed expression used, individual clones visualized. named list used, clone groups become visualization unit. clone_call identify clone. One "gene" (VDJC gene segment), "nt" (CDR3 nucleotide sequence), "aa" (CDR3 amino acid sequence, default), "strict" (VDJC gene + CDR3 nucleotide), custom column name present data. chain TCR/BCR chain(s) include. One "" (default, chains combined), "TRA", "TRB", "TRG", "TRD", "IGH", "IGL", "IGK". "", dual-chain data (e.g., TRA TRB) combined. values_by metric plot y-axis. One "count" (default, number cells per clone), "fraction" (proportion cells per clone within group), "n" (equivalent "count"). See Value types section. plot_type type plot generate. One "bar" (default), "box", "violin", "heatmap", "pies", "chord" ("circos"), "sankey" ("alluvial"), "trend", "col". See Plot types section guidance. group_by column name metadata use grouping cells (x-axis categories). Default: "Sample". single group_by column supported. groups specific groups (levels group_by) include. Default: NULL (groups included). named vector, names used display labels (e.g. c(B = "P17B", L = "P17L") renames "P17B" "B"). "chord"/"circos", exactly 2 groups required. "box", "violin", "heatmap", "pies", "sankey", "trend", least 2 groups required. subgroup_by column name metadata subgrouping. Interpretation varies plot type: "box"/"violin", controls fill grouping; "heatmap" "pies", defines pie chart composition; "heatmap" without "pies", colors row labels. supported "bar", "trend", "col". Default: NULL. subgroups specific subgroups (levels subgroup_by) include. Default: NULL (subgroups included). vector, subgroups applied groups. named list, different subgroups can specified per group (names match group_by levels). order list specifying order levels group_by. Default: NULL (uses order present data). Lower priority groups. within_subgroup Whether clone selection (clones) performed within subgroup separately. Default: TRUE plot types, FALSE "pies". TRUE, clone selectors like top(10) select top 10 clones within subgroup rather across subgroups combined. relabel Whether relabel clone IDs "clone1", "clone2", etc., ordered descending clone size. Default: TRUE "col", "chord", "circos" plot types; FALSE otherwise. Useful clone IDs long CDR3 sequences. applies visualizing individual clones (clone groups). facet_by column name facet plot separate panels. Default: NULL. supported "col", "heatmap", "pies" plot types (use split_by instead). split_by column name split plot separate subplots (via patchwork). Default: NULL. Unlike facet_by, splitting creates independent plots can different scales. y y-axis variable. Default: NULL (auto-determined values_by). "bar" plots, can "TotalSize" (total cells selected clones) "Count" (number selected clones). xlab Custom x-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated). ylab Custom y-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated based values_by: "Clone Size", "Relative Abundance", "Number Clones"). ... Additional arguments passed underlying plot function plotthis. example: "bar": see plotthis::BarPlot() (e.g. position, palette) "box": see plotthis::BoxPlot() (e.g. add_box, comparison) "violin": see plotthis::ViolinPlot() (e.g. add_box, comparison) "heatmap" "pies": see plotthis::Heatmap() (e.g. palette, show_row_names) "sankey": see plotthis::SankeyPlot() (e.g. flow, node_palette) "trend": see plotthis::TrendPlot() (e.g. line_type, palette) "chord": see plotthis::ChordPlot() "col": see plotthis::BarPlot() (used internally faceting) Additional arguments "col" plot include fill_by, fill_name, facet_scale, facet_ncol, x_text_angle, aspect.ratio, legend.position, theme_args.

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
