title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: ClonalStatPlot requires least 2 groups "box", "violin", "heatmap", "pies", "sankey", "trend" plot types. "bar" "col" work single group. "chord"/"circos" plots limited exactly 2 groups. groups, use "sankey" instead. facet_by supported "col", "heatmap", "pies" plot types plots use internal faceting. Use split_by alternative creating separate subplots. subgroup_by supported "bar", "trend", "col" plot types. using clone groups (named list clones), relabel argument effect since group names used directly. Clone selection expressions evaluated data filtered specified groups. reference group names expression (e.g., "sel(P17B > 10)"), ensure groups included groups differ display groups. "pies" plots, within_subgroup defaults FALSE, meaning clone selection occurs across subgroups combined. Set TRUE select clones within subgroup independently.

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: clones argument accepts three forms: Character vector clone IDs Directly specifies clones track. Clone IDs matched column identified clone_call (e.g., CDR3 amino acid sequences clone_call = "aa"). Selection expression (single string parentheses) string containing clone selector function call, e.g. "top(10)", "shared(P17B, P17L, group_by = 'Sample')", "sel(P17L > 10 & P17B > 0, group_by = 'Sample')". expression parsed evaluated within data context. Available selectors include: top(n, ...) — select n largest clones (total count) sel(expr, ...) — select clones matching logical expression shared(g1, g2, ...) — select clones present specified groups uniq(g1, g2, ...) — select clones unique group 1 gt(g1, g2), lt(g1, g2), eq(g1, g2), etc. — comparison-based selection selectors accept group_by, top, order, within, output_within arguments. See CloneSelectors complete documentation. Named list expressions Defines clone groups. element selection expression (), element name becomes group label. example: list(ExpandedInA = "sel(> 20, group_by = 'Sample')", ExpandedInB = "sel(B > 20, group_by = 'Sample')"). mode, visualization aggregates clones within group rather showing individual clones. default, clone selection operates within faceting/splitting group (.e., top(3) selects top 3 clones per facet). Pass group_by explicitly within selector expression change behavior.

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: ClonalStatPlot supports ten plot types, suited different analytical questions: "bar" (default) Stacked grouped bar plot showing total abundance (size fraction) selected clone across groups. Best comparing composition top clones conditions. Requires least 1 group. "box" Box plot showing distribution individual clone sizes within group. Useful assessing whether clone size distributions differ conditions. Optionally colored subgroup_by. "violin" Violin plot alternative box plot, showing full density distribution clone sizes. Supports subgroup_by split violins. "heatmap" Heatmap rows clones (clone groups) columns groups group_by. Cell color encodes clone abundance. subgroup_by provided, rows split group colored subgroup. facet_by supported; use split_by instead. "pies" Heatmap variant cell contains pie chart showing composition clone (clone group) across subgroup_by levels. pie size reflects total abundance. subgroup_by required. within_subgroup defaults FALSE plot type. "chord" / "circos" Chord diagram showing clone flow exactly 2 groups. Clones represented arcs, ribbons indicating shared clones. 2 groups, use "sankey" instead. "sankey" / "alluvial" Sankey (alluvial) diagram showing clone dynamics across groups. Flows colored clone groups (using clone groups) individual clones. Best tracking clone expansion, contraction, sharing across multiple time points conditions. "trend" Line plot showing abundance trajectory clone (clone group) across groups. Lines colored clone identity. Best longitudinal data dose-response experiments group order meaningful. "col" Column plot clone gets column, faceted group_by. Unlike "bar", places clones x-axis. facet_by supported; use split_by instead. Clones auto-relabeled default.

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
text: values_by parameter controls plotted y-axis: "count" sum cell counts clone within group (.e., clone size). default. "fraction" fraction cells belonging clone, calculated clone's cell count divided total cells group. Suitable group sizes differ proportions meaningful absolute counts. "n" number distinct clones (cells) meeting selection criteria. Shorthand "count" produces result.

title: Visualize clone abundance, frequency, and dynamics across groups — ClonalStatPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,     samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B","P19L", "P20B", "P20L")) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Type",     variables = factor(rep(c("B", "L"), 4), levels = c("L", "B")) ) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Subject",     variables = rep(c("P17", "P18", "P19", "P20"), each = 2) ) # add a fake variable (e.g. cell type from scRNA-seq) data <- lapply(data, function(x) {     x$CellType <- factor(         sample(c("CD4", "CD8", "B", "NK"), nrow(x), replace = TRUE),        levels = c("CD8", "CD4", "B", "NK")     )     return(x) }) # showing the top 10 clones (by default) ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", title = "Top 10 clones")  # showing the top 10 clones in P17B and in P17L, with the clones relabeled ClonalStatPlot(data, clones = "top(10, group_by = 'Sample')", group_by = "Sample",     groups = c("P17B", "P17L"), relabel = TRUE, values_by = "fraction",     title = "Top 10 clones in P17B and in P17L (relabelled)")  # showing the top 10 clones in each sample using violin plots ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample",     plot_type = "violin", clones = "top(10, group_by = 'Sample')",     subgroup_by = "CellType", subgroups = c("CD4", "CD8"), add_box = TRUE,     comparison = TRUE, title = "Violin plots showing top 10 clones in each sample") #> Warning: [Box/Violin/BeeswarmPlot] Some pairwise comparisons may fail due to insufficient data points or variability. Adjusting data to ensure valid comparisons.  # showing selected clones in P17B and P17L ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = c("CVVSDNTGGFKTIF_CASSVRRERANTGELFF", "NA_CASSVRRERANTGELFF"),     title = "Selected clones in P17B and P17L")  # facetting is supported, note that selection of clones is done within each facet ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Subject", groups = c("P17", "P19"),     facet_by = "Type", relabel = TRUE,     title = "Top 10 clones in Type B and L for P17 and P19")  # as well as splitting ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Subject", groups = c("P17", "P19"),     split_by = "Type", relabel = TRUE)  # showing top 10 shared clones between P17B and P17L ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = "shared(P17B, P17L, group_by = 'Sample', top = 10)", relabel = TRUE,     title = "Shared clones between P17B and P17L")  # showing clones larger than 10 in P17L and ordered by the clone size in P17L descendingly ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),      clones = "sel(P17B > 10, group_by = 'Sample', top = 5, order = desc(P17B))",      relabel = TRUE, position = "stack", title = "Top 5 clones larger than 10 in P17B")  # using trend plot ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = "sel(P17L > 10 & P17B > 0, group_by = 'Sample')", relabel = TRUE,     plot_type = "trend", title = "Clones larger than 10 in P17L and existing in P17B")  # using heatmap ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = "sel(P17L > 10 & P17B > 0, group_by = 'Sample')", relabel = TRUE,     plot_type = "heatmap", show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE,     title = "Clones larger than 10 in P17L and existing in P17B (heatmap)")  # using pies with subgroups for groups of clones ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = list(         ExpandedClonesInP17L = "sel(P17L > 20, group_by = 'Sample')",         ExpandedClonesInP17B = "sel(P17B > 20, group_by = 'Sample')"     ), subgroup_by = "CellType", pie_size = sqrt,     plot_type = "pies", show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE,     title = "Clones larger than 20 in P17L and P17B (pies with subgroups by CellType)")  # using heatmap with subgroups for groups of clones ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),    clones = list(        ExpandedClonesInP17L = "sel(P17L > 20, group_by = 'Sample')",        ExpandedClonesInP17B = "sel(P17B > 20, group_by = 'Sample')"    ), subgroup_by = "CellType", pie_size = sqrt, within_subgroup = FALSE,    plot_type = "heatmap", show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE,    title = "Clones larger than 20 in P17L and P17B (pies with subgroups by CellType)")  # using clone groups and showing dynamics using sankey plot ClonalStatPlot(data, group_by = "Sample", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     clones = list(       "Hyper-expanded clones in P17B" = "sel(P17B > 10, group_by = 'Sample')",       "Hyper-expanded clones in P17L" = "sel(P17L > 10, group_by = 'Sample')"     ), plot_type = "sankey", title = "Hyper-expanded clones in P17B and P17L")  # col plot ClonalStatPlot(data, clones = "top(5, group_by = 'Sample')", plot_type = "col",     title = "Top 5 clones in each sample (col plot)")  ClonalStatPlot(data, clones = "top(5, group_by = 'Sample')", plot_type = "col",     values_by = "fraction", facet_scale = "free",     title = "Top 5 clones in each sample (col plot, showing fraction)")  ClonalStatPlot(data, plot_type = "col", groups = c("P17B", "P17L"),     facet_ncol = 1, legend.position = "right",     relabel = TRUE, fill_by = ".Clones", fill_name = "Clones")  # Rename groups ClonalStatPlot(data, plot_type = "col", groups = c(P17B = "B", P17L = "L"),     facet_ncol = 1, legend.position = "right",     relabel = TRUE, fill_by = ".Clones", fill_name = "Clones")  # }

title: Clonal Volume Plot — ClonalVolumePlot
text: Visualizes number (fraction) unique T-cell B-cell clones across samples metadata groups. Clonal volume — count distinct clonotypes detected sample — fundamental measure immune repertoire diversity. Higher clonal volume indicates diverse repertoire, lower volume may reflect clonal expansion response antigen stimulation. ClonalVolumePlot computes clonal counts via scRepertoire::clonalQuant() visualizes bar, box, violin plots. accepts scRepertoire combined TCR/BCR data Seurat objects clonal information integrated via scRepertoire::combineExpression().

title: Clonal Volume Plot — ClonalVolumePlot
code: ClonalVolumePlot(   data,   clone_call = "aa",   chain = "both",   scale = FALSE,   plot_type = c("bar", "box", "violin"),   x = "Sample",   group_by = NULL,   facet_by = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   order = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   ... )

title: Clonal Volume Plot — ClonalVolumePlot
text: data product scRepertoire::combineTCR(), scRepertoire::combineBCR(), scRepertoire::combineExpression(). clone_call define clone. One : "gene" — V(D)JC gene combination "nt" — CDR3 nucleotide sequence "aa" — CDR3 amino acid sequence (default) "strict" — V(D)JC gene + CDR3 nucleotide custom variable name data. chain chain(s) use: "" (default), "TRA", "TRB", "TRD", "TRG", "IGH", "IGL". scale Logical; TRUE, values scaled clone proportion (fraction unique clones) instead absolute clone counts. Default FALSE. plot_type visualization type. One "bar" (default), "box", "violin". "box" "violin", data broken Sample grouped group_by show per-sample distributions. x metadata column used x-axis. Default "Sample". group_by Metadata column used group (color) data. Default NULL. facet_by Metadata column used facet plot separate panels. Default NULL. split_by Metadata column used split data separate plots. Default NULL. order named list controlling order factor levels. List names column names; list values desired order. Default NULL (use existing factor levels alphabetical order). ylab Y-axis label. Default NULL, auto-generates "Number Unique Clones" "Fraction Unique Clones" depending scale. ... Additional arguments passed underlying plotthis function: "bar" — plotthis::BarPlot() (position, palette, fill_by, ...) "box" — plotthis::BoxPlot() (comparisons, alpha, palette, ...) "violin" — plotthis::ViolinPlot() (add_box, comparisons, palette, ...)

title: Clonal Volume Plot — ClonalVolumePlot
text: ggplot object, list ggplot objects combine = FALSE passed via ....

title: Clonal Volume Plot — ClonalVolumePlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") data <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Type",     variables = factor(sample(c("B", "L"), 8, replace = TRUE), levels = c("B", "L")) ) data <- scRepertoire::addVariable(data,     variable.name = "Sex",     variables = factor(sample(c("M", "F"), 8, replace = TRUE), levels = c("M", "F")) )  ClonalVolumePlot(data)  ClonalVolumePlot(data, x = "Type")  ClonalVolumePlot(data, x = "Type", order = list(Type = c("L", "B")))  ClonalVolumePlot(data, x = c("Type", "Sex"), scale = TRUE, fill_by = "Type") #> Multiple columns are provided in 'x'. They will be concatenated into one column.  ClonalVolumePlot(data, x = "Type", group_by = "Sex", position = "stack")  ClonalVolumePlot(data,     plot_type = "box", x = "Type", comparisons = TRUE,     group_by = "Sex" ) #> Warning: [Box/Violin/BeeswarmPlot] Some pairwise comparisons may fail due to insufficient data points or variability. Adjusting data to ensure valid comparisons.  ClonalVolumePlot(data, plot_type = "violin", x = "Type", add_box = TRUE)   # on a Seurat object data(scRep_example, package = "scRepertoire") data(contig_list, package = "scRepertoire") combined <- scRepertoire::combineTCR(contig_list,     samples = c("P17B", "P17L", "P18B", "P18L", "P19B", "P19L", "P20B", "P20L") ) sobj <- scRepertoire::combineExpression(combined, scRep_example) ClonalVolumePlot(sobj) #> Warning: The 'Sample' column is not found in the meta data, 'orig.indent' will be used instead.  ClonalVolumePlot(sobj, x = "seurat_clusters") #> Warning: The 'Sample' column is not found in the meta data, 'orig.indent' will be used instead.  ClonalVolumePlot(sobj, group_by = "seurat_clusters") #> Warning: The 'Sample' column is not found in the meta data, 'orig.indent' will be used instead. what: Visualize cluster stability across clustering resolutions — ClustreePlot
title: Visualize cluster stability across clustering resolutions — ClustreePlot
text: clustree plot visualizes cells move clusters clustering performed different resolutions. resolution level column nodes, edges show flow cells clusters adjacent resolutions. essential diagnostic single-cell analysis, helping researchers choose appropriate clustering resolution revealing clusters stable (persistent across resolutions) transient (appear specific resolutions). function wrapper around plotthis::ClustreePlot() automatically extracts metadata Seurat objects. data frames, data passed directly plotthis.

title: Visualize cluster stability across clustering resolutions — ClustreePlot
code: ClustreePlot(object, ...)

title: Visualize cluster stability across clustering resolutions — ClustreePlot

Seurat object provided, object@meta.data extracted automatically passed plotthis::ClustreePlot(). data frame, must contain columns clustering assignments different resolutions, identified prefix argument. ... Additional arguments passed plotthis::ClustreePlot(). Key arguments include: prefix (Required) character string identifying columns contain cluster assignments. columns starting prefix used. standard Seurat output, "RNA_snn_res.". flip Logical. TRUE, flips tree orientation. Default: FALSE. split_by Column name(s) split data generate separate clustree plots group. Default: NULL. palette Color palette cluster nodes. Default: "Paired". edge_palette Color palette edges (transitions). Default: "Spectral". combine Logical. title: Visualize cluster stability across clustering resolutions — ClustreePlot
text: Stable clusters appear nodes predominantly single-color incoming edges (cells one source cluster) single-color outgoing edges (cells moving one target cluster). represent robust biological groupings. Transient clusters show multi-color edges, indicating arise splitting multiple parent clusters contribute multiple child clusters — sign -clustering. Node size reflects number cells cluster resolution. Edge transparency reflects proportion cells following path. Choose resolution major clusters stabilized (edges predominantly single-color) new clusters begin fragmenting.

what: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: Gene set enrichment analysis identifies biological pathways, gene ontologies, functional categories statistically -represented among list genes interest (e.g., differentially expressed genes single-cell RNA-seq experiment). Rather interpreting individual genes isolation, enrichment analysis places gene-level results broader biological context, revealing processes, functions, diseases perturbed. EnrichmentPlot generates publication-quality visualizations enrichment results across eight distinct plot types, suited different analytical perspective: bar — Horizontal bar chart top enriched terms, ordered significance. Best quick overview showing small number terms. dot — Dot plot x-axis shows continuous metric (default: GeneRatio), dot size reflects gene count, dot color reflects significance. Ideal comparing terms along two dimensions simultaneously. lollipop — Lollipop chart combining dot bar aesthetics. Similar dot plot stems emphasizing ranking. comparison — Side--side dot plot comparing enrichment across groups (e.g., cell types, conditions). Requires group_by. network — Network visualization nodes enriched terms edges represent overlapping gene sets. Reveals functional modules redundant terms. enrichmap — Enrichment map similar network plot optimized large term sets (default top_term = 100). Nodes terms edges represent gene overlap. wordcloud — Word cloud term size reflects significance. Can display either enrichment terms (word_type = "term") individual gene symbols (word_type = "feature"). heatmap — Heatmap enrichment significance across groups (group_by mapped columns). Useful comparing enrichment patterns across multiple conditions cell types. function auto-detects input data format (clusterProfiler enrichR) delegates visualization appropriate plotthis plotting function.

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
code: EnrichmentPlot(   data,   top_term = NULL,   plot_type = c("bar", "dot", "lollipop", "network", "enrichmap", "wordcloud",     "comparison", "heatmap"),   x_by = NULL,   size_by = NULL,   fill_cutoff_name = NULL,   fill_name = NULL,   values_fill = 0,   character_width = 50,   expand = NULL,   word_type = c("term", "feature"),   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   facet_by = NULL,   facet_scales = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   group_by_sep = "_",   metric = "p.adjust",   cutoff = NULL,   palette = "Spectral",   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   ... )

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: data data frame enrichment results. Must output clusterProfiler function (enrichGO, enrichKEGG, enrichPathway, enrichWP, etc.) enrichR result processed plotthis::prepare_enrichr_result(). function auto-detects format based column names. top_term Integer. Number top terms (significance) display per group/facet combination. Default: 6 plot types except "enrichmap" defaults 100. Note terms filtered globally — top terms selected independently within combination split_by, group_by, facet_by levels. plot_type Character. type plot generate. One : "bar", "dot", "lollipop", "network", "enrichmap", "wordcloud", "comparison", "heatmap". See Description section guidance choosing plot type. Default: "bar". x_by Character. Column name(s) use x-axis. Works "dot" "lollipop" plot types. Default: NULL (defaults "GeneRatio" internally). size_by Character. Column name(s) map point size. Works "comparison", "dot", "lollipop" plot types. Default: NULL (defaults "GeneRatio" comparison, "Count" dot lollipop). fill_cutoff_name Character. Legend label terms exceed cutoff (shown gray). Applies "comparison", "dot", "lollipop" plot types. Default: NULL (defaults "Non-significant" cutoff set). fill_name Character. Legend title fill color scale (significance metric). Applies "comparison", "dot", "lollipop" plot types. Default: NULL (auto-generated "-log10(metric)"). values_fill Numeric. fill value missing entries heatmap matrix. Used "heatmap" plot type. Default: 0. character_width Integer. Maximum character width term descriptions line-wrapping. Applies plot types; "heatmap" wrapping deferred Heatmap function. Default: 50. expand Numeric vector length 1, 2, 4. Axis expansion factors passed plotthis::BarPlot(). Used "bar" plot type. Default: NULL (defaults c(0.1, 0.6, 0, 0.6)). word_type Character. display wordcloud. One "term" (enrichment term descriptions) "feature" (gene symbols enriched gene list). Used "wordcloud" plot type. Default: "term". split_by Character vector. Column name(s) data split data generate separate plots unique value. Multiple columns concatenated split_by_sep. Default: NULL. split_by_sep Character. Separator used concatenating multiple split_by columns. Default: "_". facet_by Character vector. Column name(s) data use faceting (generating sub-panels within plot). Default: NULL. facet_scales Character. Facet scale behavior — "fixed" (scales), "free", "free_x", "free_y". Default: NULL (defaults "free_y" bar, dot, lollipop, comparison plots). group_by Character vector. Column name(s) data group terms. Behavior depends plot_type: "comparison" — Groups shown x-axis categories dot plot comparing enrichment across groups. Required type. "heatmap" — Groups used columns heatmap (mapped columns_by plotthis::Heatmap()). types — group_by supported raise error. Use facet_by split_by instead. Multiple columns concatenated group_by_sep. Default: NULL. group_by_sep Character. Separator used concatenating multiple group_by columns. Used "comparison" plot type. Default: "_". metric Character. column name data use significance metric ordering coloring terms. Common choices "p.adjust" (default), "pvalue", "qvalue". metric p-value column, \(-log_{10}\) transformation applied automatically significant terms higher values. cutoff Numeric. significance threshold mark plot. Default: NULL (marking). behavior depends plot_type: "bar" — Adds vertical dashed line transformed cutoff (e.g., \(-log_{10}(0.05)\)). "dot", "lollipop", "comparison" — Terms cutoff colored gray legend label fill_cutoff_name. "heatmap" — Adds asterisk (*) labels cells metric exceeds cutoff. "network", "enrichmap", "wordcloud" — effect. parameter marks terms — filter . Use top_term control many terms shown. palette Character. Color palette name fill scale. See plotthis::show_palettes() available palettes. Default: "Spectral". xlab Character. Custom x-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated based plot type x_by/metric). ylab Character. Custom y-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated based plot type). ... Additional arguments passed underlying plotthis plotting function, determined plot_type: "bar" plotthis::BarPlot() "dot" plotthis::DotPlot() "lollipop" plotthis::LollipopPlot() "network" plotthis::EnrichNetwork() "enrichmap" plotthis::EnrichMap() "wordcloud" plotthis::WordCloudPlot() "comparison" plotthis::DotPlot() "heatmap" plotthis::Heatmap()

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object split_by generates multiple plots combine = TRUE), list ggplot objects combine = FALSE. specific return type depends underlying plotthis function dispatched plot_type.

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: function auto-detects clusterProfiler vs enrichR input format. enrichR input, must contain P.value Adjusted.P.value columns. enrichR results came different pipeline, pre-process plotthis::prepare_enrichr_result(). cutoff parameter marks terms — filter . reduce number displayed terms, use top_term. GeneRatio strings (e.g., "38/225") BgRatio strings automatically converted numeric values dividing numerator denominator. using group_by plot_type = "comparison", size_by defaults "GeneRatio" group's terms shown side--side. plot_type = "heatmap", group_by becomes heatmap columns. group_by supported "bar", "dot", "lollipop", "network", "enrichmap", "wordcloud" — use facet_by split_by separate groups types. plot_type = "wordcloud" word_type = "feature", individual gene symbols extracted geneID column. Gene-level significance scores aggregated summing \(-log_{10}(p)\) values.

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: function auto-detects input format checking characteristic column names: clusterProfiler (enrichGO, enrichKEGG, enrichPathway, etc.) — recognized presence pvalue, p.adjust, qvalue columns. enrichR (web-based enrichment tool) — recognized presence P.value Adjusted.P.value columns. enrichR results automatically converted clusterProfiler-compatible format via plotthis's prepare_enrichr_result(). neither format detected, function stops error.

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: metric p-value column (pvalue, p.adjust, qvalue), function applies \(-log_{10}\) transformation significant terms higher values plot. transformed metric stored internally .metric. cutoff specified, also transformed (e.g., p.adjust = 0.05 becomes line \(-log_{10}(0.05) = 1.3\)). "bar", "dot", "lollipop", "comparison", "heatmap" plot types, GeneRatio (stored strings like "38/225") BgRatio automatically converted numeric ratios.

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
text: unique combination split_by, group_by, facet_by levels, function selects top_term terms smallest metric values (.e., significant). ensures facet split shows relevant terms rather globally significant ones. default top_term 6 plot types 100 "enrichmap" (benefits showing terms reveal network structure gene set relationships).

title: Visualize gene set enrichment and over-representation analysis results — EnrichmentPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data(enrich_example, package = "plotthis") enrich_example$Group <- sample(LETTERS[1:3], nrow(enrich_example), replace = TRUE) data(enrich_multidb_example, package = "plotthis")  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example)  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, cutoff = 0.05)  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, palette = "Paired")   enrich_example$Description <- enrich_example$ID EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "heatmap", group_by = "Group",  show_row_names = TRUE, show_column_names = TRUE, cutoff = 0.05)   # Multiple databases#' EnrichmentPlot(enrich_multidb_example, facet_by = "Database", facet_nrow = 2)   enrich_example$Group <- sample(c("A", "B"), nrow(enrich_example), replace = TRUE) EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "comparison", group_by = "Group")  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "dot", top_term = 10)  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "lollipop", top_term = 10)  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "network")  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "enrichmap")  EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "wordcloud")  # Wordcloud with feature EnrichmentPlot(enrich_example, plot_type = "wordcloud", word_type = "feature")  # }

what: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: central question single-cell analysis features — genes, gene signatures, module scores, molecular measurements — vary across cell types, conditions, experimental groups. FeatureStatPlot answers question providing eight complementary visualization types, suited different analytical perspective: violin — Violin plot showing full distribution feature values per identity group. Best comparing expression distributions detecting bimodality outliers. box — Box plot summarizing feature values quartiles outliers. compact alternative violin plot. bar — Bar chart aggregated feature values (default: mean) per group. Useful summary-level comparisons error bars. ridge — Ridge (joy) plot showing density curves per group. Effective comparing many groups distribution shape matters. dim — Dimensionality reduction plot (UMAP, t-SNE, PCA) cells colored feature expression. Reveals spatial patterns gene expression reduced space. cor — Correlation plot two features (scatter fitted line annotations) among multiple features (pairs plot). Reveals co-expression relationships. heatmap — Heatmap feature expression across identity groups. Supports rich annotations (row/column metadata, bar charts, pie charts, violin plots) flexible clustering. go-choice visualizing many features across many groups. dot — Dot plot (shortcut heatmap cell_type = "dot") dot size reflects fraction expressing cells dot color reflects mean expression. compact, publication-ready format marker gene visualization. function S3 generic methods Seurat objects, Giotto objects, AnnData (.h5ad) file paths, H5File objects (via hdf5r). method extracts relevant expression matrix metadata, delegates internal .feature_stat_plot() dispatches appropriate plotthis plotting function.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
code: FeatureStatPlot(   object,   features,   plot_type = c("violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dim", "cor", "heatmap", "dot"),   spat_unit = NULL,   feat_type = NULL,   downsample = NULL,   pos_only = c("no", "any", "all"),   reduction = NULL,   graph = NULL,   bg_cutoff = 0,   dims = 1:2,   rows_name = "Features",   ident = NULL,   assay = NULL,   layer = NULL,   agg = mean,   group_by = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   facet_by = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   x_text_angle = NULL,   ... )

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: object object containing single-cell expression data. Supported types: Seurat object, Giotto object, character path .h5ad file, opened H5File object hdf5r package. features character vector named list character vectors specifying features plot. Features can gene names, gene signature scores, column present expression matrix metadata. Named lists (e.g., list(Beta = c("Ins1", "Ins2"))) enable automatic row grouping heatmap dot plots. plot_type Character. type plot generate. One : "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dim", "cor", "heatmap", "dot". See Description section guidance choosing plot type. Default: "violin". spat_unit Character. spatial unit extract data . applicable Giotto objects. Default: NULL (auto-detected Giotto). feat_type Character. feature type extract (e.g., "rna", "dna", "protein"). applicable Giotto objects. Default: NULL (auto-detected Giotto). downsample Numeric. Number fraction cells downsample per identity group. Used "violin", "box", "ridge" plot types reduce overplotting large datasets: downsample > 1: Exact number cells per group. 0 < downsample <= 1: Fraction cells per group. Downsampling performed within identity group (via dplyr::slice_sample(= ident)). Default: NULL (downsampling). pos_only Character. Whether restrict cells positive feature values: "" — Include cells (default). "" — Include cells least one feature > 0. "" — Include cells features > 0. named feature lists, filtering applied flattened features. reduction Character. Name dimensionality reduction use (e.g., "umap", "tsne", "pca"). Required plot_type = "dim"; optional types reduction coordinates can used feature values. Seurat objects, defaults default reduction available. Default: NULL. graph Character TRUE. graph (nearest-neighbor network) name overlay edges connected cells dim plot. Seurat objects, name exist object@graphs. Giotto objects, name exist nearest network list. TRUE, first available graph used. used plot_type = "dim". Default: NULL (edges). bg_cutoff Numeric. Expression cutoff background dim plots. Cells expression value shown background color (typically gray). Set -Inf color cells. used plot_type = "dim". Default: 0. dims Integer vector length 2. dimensions (columns) reduction plot x y axes. used plot_type = "dim". Default: 1:2. rows_name Character. column name used identify feature rows heatmap key merging rows_data. used plot_type = "heatmap" "dot". Default: "Features". ident Character. metadata column name identifying cell groups (e.g., "CellType", "SubCellType", "seurat_clusters"). Used x-axis violin, box, bar, ridge plots; heatmap columns heatmap dot plots; grouping variable correlation plots. Seurat objects, defaults active identity ("Identity"). Required non-dim plots Giotto H5File objects. Default: NULL. assay Character. assay name extract feature data (e.g., "RNA", "SCT", "integrated"). Seurat objects, passed SeuratObject::GetAssayData(). H5File objects, determines whether read X (assay = "RNA") layers/<assay>. applicable Giotto objects. Default: NULL. layer Character. layer name within assay extract data (e.g., "data", "counts", "scale.data"). Seurat objects, passed SeuratObject::GetAssayData(). Giotto objects, passed GiottoClass::getExpression(). Default: NULL. agg Function. aggregation function applied plot_type = "bar". Common choices: mean (default), median, sum. Applied within group defined ident, group_by, split_by. group_by Character. metadata column name subdivide cells within identity group (e.g., coloring treatment within cell type). Works "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge" plot types. Default: NULL. split_by Character vector TRUE. Metadata column name(s) split data separate plots (one per unique value). TRUE, splits features , creating one plot per feature. Multiple columns concatenated. Default: NULL. facet_by Character vector. Metadata column name(s) facet data within plot. Note: "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge" plot types, facet_by always NULL plot already faceted features. Works normally "dim", "heatmap", "dot", "cor" plot types. Default: NULL. xlab Character. Custom x-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated based plot type). ylab Character. Custom y-axis label. Default: NULL (auto-generated based plot type). x_text_angle Numeric. Angle (degrees) x-axis text labels. Used "violin", "box", "bar" plot types. Default: NULL (defaults 45). ... Additional arguments passed underlying plotthis plotting function, determined plot_type: "violin" plotthis::ViolinPlot() "box" plotthis::BoxPlot() "bar" plotthis::BarPlot() "ridge" plotthis::RidgePlot() "dim" plotthis::FeatureDimPlot() "heatmap" plotthis::Heatmap() "dot" plotthis::Heatmap() cell_type = "dot" "cor" plotthis::CorPlot() (2 features) plotthis::CorPairsPlot() (3+ features) Common arguments include palette, flip, add_bg, add_point, add_box, stack, comparisons, theme, legend.position, many — see documentation specific plotthis function details.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: ggplot object (patchwork object split_by generates multiple plots combine = TRUE), list ggplot objects combine = FALSE. specific return type depends underlying plotthis function dispatched plot_type.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: See vignettes examples non-Seurat objects: Giotto Visium Giotto Xenium Working AnnData .h5ad files

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge" plot types, data automatically pivoted wide long format features faceted. use facet_by types — use split_by instead. "heatmap" "dot" plot types, named feature lists automatically converted rows_data data frame rows_split_by set. can also provide rows_data richer annotations (e.g., TF status, CSPA membership). dot plot type convenience shortcut plot_type = "heatmap" cell_type = "dot". pre-configures sensible defaults: dot size = fraction expressing cells, dot color = mean expression, reticle added, clustering. "dim" plots graph, edges drawn connected cells. helps reveal whether feature expression follows neighborhood structure (e.g., continuous gradients vs scattered expression). ident specified Seurat objects, active identity (Idents(object)) used automatically. Giotto objects require spat_unit feat_type locate correct expression matrix metadata.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: FeatureStatPlot supports four input types, handled S3 method: Seurat (FeatureStatPlot.Seurat) — Uses SeuratObject::GetAssayData() extract expression data @meta.data cell metadata. Reductions accessed via SeuratObject::Embeddings(). Giotto (FeatureStatPlot.giotto) — Uses GiottoClass::getExpression() GiottoClass::getCellMetadata(). Requires spat_unit feat_type parameters. .h5ad path (FeatureStatPlot.character) — Opens file via hdf5r, delegates FeatureStatPlot.H5File. Currently .h5ad (AnnData) files supported. H5File (FeatureStatPlot.H5File) — Reads expression X (layers/<assay>), cell metadata obs, reductions obsm.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: Features can provided : character vector — e.g., c("Sox9", "Neurog3", "Ins1"). features treated equally. named list — e.g., list(Ductal = c("Sox9", "Anxa2"), Endocrine = c("Ins1", "Gcg")). names used row group annotations heatmap dot plots (via rows_split_by), automatically creating rows_data data frame maps feature group. especially useful organizing marker genes cell type. pos_only "" "", cells filtered based whether positive values specified features. named feature lists, filter applies flattened set features.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
text: plot types ("violin", "box", "bar", "ridge"), features automatically faceted — feature appears panel. facet_by parameter therefore restricted types. Use split_by (split_by = TRUE split feature) creating separate plots per category. "dim", "heatmap", "dot", "cor" plots, features incorporated single visualization split_by/facet_by behave normally.

title: Visualize feature expression and statistics across cell groups — FeatureStatPlot
code: # \donttest{ data(pancreas_sub)  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", facet_scales = "free_y")  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", plot_type = "box", facet_scales = "free_y")  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", plot_type = "bar", facet_scales = "free_y")  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", plot_type = "ridge", flip = TRUE, facet_scales = "free_y") #> Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0498 #> Picking joint bandwidth of 516 #> Picking joint bandwidth of 0.0498 #> Picking joint bandwidth of 516  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", facet_scales = "free_y", add_point = TRUE)  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", facet_scales = "free_y", add_trend = TRUE)  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", facet_scales = "free_y", add_stat = mean)  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("G2M_score", "nCount_RNA"),    ident = "SubCellType", facet_scales = "free_y", group_by = "Phase") #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes. #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes. #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes. #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes.  FeatureStatPlot(    subset(pancreas_sub,        subset = SubCellType %in% c("Ductal", "Ngn3 low EP", "Ngn3 high EP")),    features = c("G2M_score"),    ident = "SubCellType", group_by = "Phase", comparisons = TRUE) #> Detected more than 2 groups. Use multiple_method for comparison  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("Rbp4", "Pyy"), ident = "SubCellType",    add_bg = TRUE, add_box = TRUE, stack = TRUE)  # Use `pos_only` to include only cells with positive expression of all features FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c("Rbp4", "Pyy"), ident = "SubCellType",    add_bg = TRUE, add_box = TRUE, stack = TRUE, pos_only = "all")  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, features = c(        "Sox9", "Anxa2", "Bicc1", # Ductal        "Neurog3", "Hes6", # EPs        "Fev", "Neurod1", # Pre-endocrine        "Rbp4", "Pyy", # Endocrine        "Ins1", "Gcg", "Sst", "Ghrl" # Beta, Alpha, Delta, Epsilon    ), ident = "SubCellType", add_bg = TRUE, stack = TRUE,    legend.position = "top", legend.direction = "horizontal")  FeatureStatPlot(pancreas_sub, plot_type = "box", features = c(       "Sox9", "Anxa2", "Bicc1", # title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: Visualize differential expression (DE) results — typically output Seurat::FindMarkers() Seurat::FindAllMarkers() — across variety plot types. MarkersPlot() bridges gap DE testing visualization providing unified interface summary-level DE visualizations (volcano, jitter, heatmap, dot plots fold changes significance) expression-level visualizations (violin, box, bar, ridge, heatmap, dot plots actual expression values Seurat object). function handles two broad categories plots: DE summary plots (object required): visualize DE statistics — log2 fold change, percentage difference, p-values, adjusted p-values — across groups comparisons. "volcano" / "volcano_log2fc" — Volcano plot log2 fold change x-axis \(-log_{10}(p)\) y-axis. Genes passing cutoff highlighted top genes labeled. Ideal overview effect size vs. significance. "volcano_pct" — Volcano plot percentage-point difference (pct.1 - pct.2) x-axis. Useful biological question detection rate rather expression magnitude. "jitter" / "jitter_log2fc" — Jitter plot log2 fold changes across groups (defined subset_by). Dot size encodes \(-log_{10}(p)\). Reveals distribution effect sizes per cluster condition. "jitter_pct" — Jitter plot percentage-point differences across groups. "heatmap_log2fc" — Heatmap log2 fold changes (genes × groups). Cells can marked significance via cutoff sig_mark. "heatmap_pct" — Heatmap percentage-point differences (genes × groups). significance-marking support. "dot_log2fc" — Dot plot log2 fold changes (genes × groups). Dot size encodes \(-log_{10}(p)\). "dot_pct" — Dot plot percentage-point differences (genes × groups). Dot size encodes \(-log_{10}(p)\). Expression plots (object required): visualize actual expression values selected marker genes context original Seurat object. useful validating DE results inspecting underlying expression distributions. "heatmap" — Expression heatmap selected marker genes. "violin" — Violin plots expression per gene. "box" — Box plots expression per gene. "bar" — Bar plots mean expression per gene. "ridge" — Ridge plots expression distribution per gene. "dot" — Dot plot expression (fraction expressing × mean expression) per gene.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
code: MarkersPlot(   markers,   object = NULL,   plot_type = c("volcano", "volcano_log2fc", "volcano_pct", "jitter", "jitter_log2fc",     "jitter_pct", "heatmap_log2fc", "heatmap_pct", "dot_log2fc", "dot_pct", "heatmap",     "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dot"),   subset_by = NULL,   subset_as_facet = FALSE,   comparison_by = NULL,   p_adjust = TRUE,   cutoff = NULL,   show_labels = FALSE,   sig_mark = "*",   order_by = NULL,   select = ifelse(plot_type %in% c("volcano", "volcano_log2fc", "volcano_pct",     "jitter", "jitter_log2fc", "jitter_pct"), 5, 10),   ... )

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: markers data frame differential expression results, typically output Seurat::FindMarkers() Seurat::FindAllMarkers(). Must contain columns "gene" (gene symbols rownames), "p_val", "avg_log2FC". percentage-based plots (volcano_pct, jitter_pct, heatmap_pct, dot_pct), columns "pct.1" "pct.2" also required. object Seurat object. Required expression-based plot types: "heatmap", "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dot". used DE summary plot types. Default: NULL. plot_type type plot generate. One "volcano", "volcano_log2fc", "volcano_pct", "jitter", "jitter_log2fc", "jitter_pct", "heatmap_log2fc", "heatmap_pct", "dot_log2fc", "dot_pct", "heatmap", "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dot". See Description details type. subset_by column name markers indicating grouping marker identified (e.g., cluster column FindAllMarkers()). Supports "marker_column:metadata_column" syntax linking Seurat object metadata (see Metadata column mapping section). jitter DE heatmap/dot plot types, subset_by required defines x-axis column groups. expression plot types, subset_by controls faceting splitting. Default: NULL. subset_as_facet Logical. TRUE, facet plot subset_by groups instead splitting separate plots. useful expression plot types. volcano plots, controls whether faceting split_by dispatch used. Default: FALSE. comparison_by column name markers indicating comparison (e.g., "g1:g2" pairwise comparison, single group name one-vs-rest). Required expression-based plot types ("heatmap", "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dot"). Supports "marker_column:metadata_column" syntax (see Metadata column mapping section). comparison values contain colon (e.g., "G2M:G1"), two groups either side colon used subset object. single group present, one-vs-comparison assumed. Default: NULL. p_adjust Logical. TRUE (default), use adjusted p-value (p_val_adj column) significance calculations y-axis transformations. FALSE, use raw p-value (p_val column). cutoff Numeric. p-value (adjusted p-value, depending p_adjust) threshold labeling significance. volcano plots, sets y_cutoff. heatmap-based DE plots (heatmap_log2fc, heatmap_pct), controls cells receive significance marks. Default: NULL (cutoff; defaults 0.05 volcano plots). show_labels Logical. heatmap_log2fc heatmap_pct plot types . TRUE, display numeric values heatmap cells. combined cutoff, values significance marks shown. Default: FALSE. sig_mark Character. symbol compound mark used annotate statistically significant cells heatmap_log2fc heatmap_pct plots. Must valid ComplexHeatmap mark: single characters ("-", "|", "+", "/", "\\", "x", "o") compound marks ("[*]", "<*>", "(*)", "{*}"). Note "*" conflicts show_labels = TRUE use label layer — use compound mark instead. Default: "*". order_by string expression order markers (evaluated dplyr::arrange()). Can reference columns markers well columns object metadata (object provided subset_by enables merging). first value merged metadata columns used. Example: "desc(avg_log2FC)". ordering affects markers selected select numeric. Default: NULL. select select markers labeling display. See Marker selection filtering section full details. Numeric: Top N markers per subset_by group (default: 5 volcano/jitter types, 10 others). Character expression: Filter condition dplyr::filter(). Character vector: Multiple filter expressions; containing subset_by column name filter overall data, others filter within remaining data. ... Additional arguments passed underlying plotting function, depending plot_type: volcano, volcano_log2fc, volcano_pct Passed plotthis::VolcanoPlot(). Common arguments: x_cutoff, x_cutoff_name, label_by, color_by, nlabel, flip_negative. jitter, jitter_log2fc, jitter_pct Passed plotthis::JitterPlot(). Common arguments: add_hline, shape, size_by, nlabel. heatmap_log2fc, heatmap_pct, dot_log2fc, dot_pct Passed plotthis::Heatmap(). Common arguments: show_row_names, show_column_names, values_fill, palette, cluster_rows, cluster_columns, add_reticle. heatmap, violin, box, bar, ridge, dot Passed FeatureStatPlot. Common arguments: name, palette, ncol, nrow, stack, columns_split_by.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: ggplot object (plotthis::VolcanoPlot() plotthis::JitterPlot()), Heatmap object (plotthis::Heatmap()), ggplot/patchwork object (FeatureStatPlot). split_by faceting generates multiple plots combine = TRUE (default), combined patchwork object returned; combine = FALSE, list individual plots returned.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: subset_by required jitter plots ("jitter", "jitter_log2fc", "jitter_pct") DE heatmap/dot plots ("heatmap_log2fc", "heatmap_pct", "dot_log2fc", "dot_pct"). Without , grouping axis. comparison_by required expression-based plot types ("heatmap", "violin", "box", "bar", "ridge", "dot") — tells function comparison groups extract object. object provided subset_by maps metadata column, markers data frame left-joined object metadata. first row per group kept non-key columns, sufficient annotation purposes can cause issues per-cell metadata needed. expression-based heatmap dot plots, subset_by_2 available (.e., metadata column mapped), genes automatically grouped subset_by via columns_split_by, group_by set NULL. function calculates \(-log_{10}(p)\) (\(-log_{10}(p_{adj})\)) internally stores temporary neg_log10_p column. column available use order_by. comparison involves single group (one-vs-rest), cells comparison group labeled "" object metadata.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: subset_by comparison_by support special "marker_column:metadata_column" syntax linking columns markers data frame columns Seurat object's metadata. part colon refers column markers. part colon refers column object@meta.data. one name provided (colon), used markers column metadata column (matching metadata column exists). Example: subset_by = "cluster:RNA_snn_res.0.8" maps cluster column DE results RNA_snn_res.0.8 column Seurat metadata. markers data frame object metadata merged via subset_by, first value non-key column within group retained — design avoid duplication.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: select argument supports three modes: Numeric — Select top N markers (ordered order_by) within group defined subset_by. volcano jitter plots, markers plotted top N per group labeled. plot types, selected markers shown. Single expression — filter expression string evaluated dplyr::filter(). example, "p_val_adj < 0.05 & avg_log2FC > 1". markers matching condition retained across groups. Multiple expressions (character vector) — element evaluated independently. Expressions mention subset_by column filter overall data (removing groups); expressions filter within remaining data. example, select = c("cluster %% c('0', '1')", "p_val_adj < 0.05") first restricts clusters 0 1, keeps significant markers. numeric string like "5" among expressions treated top-N selection. Default select: 5 volcano jitter plot types, 10 plot types.

title: Visualize differential expression markers — MarkersPlot
text: heatmap_log2fc heatmap_pct, cutoff sig_mark arguments control statistically significant cells annotated heatmap: cutoff set show_labels = FALSE, cells p-value (adjusted p-value) cutoff marked sig_mark using ComplexHeatmap's mark system. Valid sig_mark values include "-", "|", "+", "/", "\\", "x", "o", compound marks like "[*]", "<*>", "(*)", "{*}". cutoff set show_labels = TRUE, numeric values significance marks displayed (cell_type = "label+mark"). Note sig_mark = "*" work show_labels = TRUE — use compound marks instead. cutoff = NULL show_labels = TRUE, cells labeled numeric values.

what: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
title: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
text: SCPlotterChat R6 class provides conversational interface generating single-cell data visualizations natural language. leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) via tidyprompt package interpret user requests, select appropriate visualization functions, identify relevant data objects, generate executable R code — without requiring user know function names, parameter details, data formats.

title: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
text: SCPlotterChat orchestrates three-stage LLM pipeline user request: Stage 1 — Tool identification (locate_tool): user's prompt analyzed registry exported scplotter functions (built automatically package documentation). LLM selects appropriate visualization function, considering current conversation history context (e.g., "heatmap instead" reuses previous tool). Stage 2 — Data identification (locate_data): LLM identifies target data object available sources: Data frames Seurat objects global environment Datasets exported scplotter, Seurat, SeuratObject, scRepertoire Manually set data via $set_data() Stage 3 — Code generation execution (run_tool): LLM generates R code call selected visualization function identified data object. code evaluated isolated environment, resulting plot returned. Validation ensures generated code syntactically valid R.

title: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
text: SCPlotterChat maintains conversation history across calls $ask(). interaction records user's prompt, selected tool, data object, generated R code. enables contextual follow-requests "change palette Spectral" "facet condition" — LLM understands refinements previous plot rather new independent requests.

title: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
text: initialization, SCPlotterChat scans scplotter package namespace exported functions parses Rd documentation build tool registry. tool entry contains function's title, description, usage signature, arguments (descriptions), usage examples. ... arguments reference package functions (e.g., [plotthis::Heatmap()]), documentation recursively expanded include functions' arguments well. Two special pseudo-tools also registered: ListTools — Lists available visualization functions ListData — Lists available data objects LLM selects either , chat responds listing rather generating plot.

title: LLM-Powered Chat Interface for Single-Cell Visualization — SCPlotterChat
text: SCPlotterChat requires LLM provider object tidyprompt package. provider supported tidyprompt can used: OpenAI, DeepSeek, Ollama (local), others. See https://tjarkvandemerwe.github.io/tidyprompt/articles/getting_started.html#setup--llm-provider setup instructions.

code: # \donttest{ if (FALSE) { # Setup LLM provider (requires an API key) provider <- tidyprompt::llm_provider_openai(     parameters = list(model = "deepseek-v4-flash", stream = TRUE),     url = "https://api.deepseek.com/chat/completions",     api_key = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Create chat instance chat <- SCPlotterChat$new(provider)  # List available tools chat$ask("What are the tools to use?") # Tool identified:  ListTools # Available tools: # -  CCCPlot :  Cell-Cell Communication Plot #    Visualizes ligand-receptor interaction inference results ... # ...  # List available data chat$ask("What data is available?")  # Generate a plot from natural language chat$ask("Plot the default cell-cell communication plot for the cellphonedb_res dataset") # Tool identified:  CCCPlot # Data object identified:  cellphonedb_res # Code ran: CCCPlot(cellphonedb_res)  # Refine with conversational context chat$ask("do a heatmap instead") # Tool identified:  CCCPlot # Data object identified:  cellphonedb_res # Code ran: CCCPlot(cellphonedb_res, plot_type = "heatmap")  # Add a title chat$ask("Add a proper title to the plot")  # Manually set data to avoid auto-detection chat$set_data(scplotter::cellphonedb_res) chat$ask("Make a dot plot")  # Inspect conversation history chat$get_history()  # Clear history for a fresh conversation chat$clear_history() } # }

what: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: Plot categorical metadata — cluster identities, tissue regions, sample labels, discrete grouping variable — directly spatial tissue coordinates. SpatDimPlot() spatial analogue UMAP/t-SNE plot colored cluster: spot, cell, molecule colored group membership, making easy assess spatial organization cell types, anatomical regions, experimental conditions. continuous features (gene expression, dimension reduction scores), use SpatFeaturePlot instead.

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
code: SpatDimPlot(   object,   fov = NULL,   boundaries = NULL,   image = NULL,   masks = NULL,   shapes = NULL,   points = NULL,   ext = NULL,   crop = TRUE,   group_by = NULL,   features = NULL,   layer = NULL,   scale_factor = NULL,   layers = NULL,   flip_y = NULL,   padding = NULL,   image_scale = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   nmols = 1000,   shapes_fill_by = NULL,   graph = NULL,   shape = 16,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   theme = "theme_box",   theme_args = list(),   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   feat_type = "rna",   use_overlap = FALSE,   shapes_feat_type = "cell",   shapes_alpha = NULL,   spat_unit = NULL,   spat_loc_name = NULL,   spat_enr_names = NULL,   ... )

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: object Seurat object (spatial data) Giotto object. S3 methods dispatch appropriate spatial technology handler. fov name field view (FOV) plot. Seurat FOV-based objects (Xenium, CosMx, etc.), defaults SeuratObject::DefaultFOV(). applicable Visium Slide-seq objects. boundaries name segmentation boundaries within FOV use cell outlines. Seurat FOV-based objects, defaults SeuratObject::DefaultBoundary(). applicable Visium Slide-seq objects. image Controls image/background layer. Possible values: NULL — Default behavior: Visium, first image used; Giotto FOV objects, image plotted. character string naming image object — specific image plotted. color name (e.g., "white", "lightgray") — fills background solid color rectangle. TRUE — Visium: uses first image. Giotto FOV: plots non-overlapping images. Seurat FOV: raises error (single default image). FALSE — Disables image layer entirely. masks Logical. Whether plot cell segmentation masks. Currently supported — setting TRUE produce error object types. shapes Controls cell boundary (shapes) layer. Possible values: TRUE — Seurat FOV objects, uses boundaries shape boundaries. object types, uses default boundaries. supported Visium Slide-seq objects. character string — name specific set boundaries within FOV (Seurat) spatial info name (Giotto) use shapes. FALSE — Disables shapes layer. Defaults TRUE shapes_fill_by provided, NULL otherwise. points Logical. Whether plot points layer (cells, spots, molecules points spatial coordinates). Default: TRUE. ext spatial extent (bounding box) plot. NULL, extent calculated automatically data , crop = TRUE, tissue coordinates. Can numeric vector format c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) terra::SpatExtent object. crop Logical. Whether crop plot extent tissue/spots. TRUE (default), plot automatically zoomed data extent optional padding. Analogous crop argument Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(). group_by metadata column name used color points. Must character factor column object's metadata. SpatDimPlot(), NULL object FOV data features, defaults "molecules"; otherwise defaults "Identity" (active cluster identities). SpatFeaturePlot(), group_by ignored — use SpatDimPlot categorical grouping. special value "molecules" enables molecule-level visualization FOV-based data. features character vector feature names visualize. SpatFeaturePlot(), feature plotted separate facet (combined theme single feature given), expression values coloring points. SpatDimPlot(), features treated molecule names plot single-molecule resolution (FOV data ). Can include gene names, metadata column names, dimension reduction components. layer assay layer extract feature expression values. Seurat objects, one "data" (default), "scale.data", "counts". Giotto objects, one "normalized" (default), "scaled", "raw", "counts", "custom". scale_factor Internal use. image scale factor extracted object, used map pixel tissue coordinate spaces. Automatically determined object's image data. layers character vector specifying layers include order. Possible values "image", "masks", "shapes", "points". Order matters — first element drawn first (bottom). Omit layer name disable . Default: c("image", "masks", "shapes", "points") intersected layers non-FALSE/non-NULL. flip_y Logical. Whether flip y-axis. primarily internal coordinate system alignment — Visium/Slide-seq objects default TRUE, FOV-based objects FALSE. cases need set manually. padding Numeric. Extra space added around data extent crop = TRUE. Expressed fraction data range (e.g., 0.05 adds 5\ defaults 0. object types, defaults 0 image layer present 0.05 otherwise. image_scale image scale factor name use, typically "lowres" "hires". Controls resolution stored image rendered. Analogous image.scale argument Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(). x Internal use. name x-coordinate column spatial data. Auto-detected based spatial technology ("imagerow" Visium V1, varies types). y Internal use. name y-coordinate column spatial data. Auto-detected based spatial technology ("imagecol" Visium V1, varies types). nmols Integer. Maximum number molecules plot per feature group_by = "molecules". Analogous nmols argument Seurat::ImageDimPlot(). Default: 1000. shapes_fill_by column name metadata (feature/gene name) used fill cell boundary shapes. single color string provided, shapes filled color. set, shapes defaults TRUE. graph name spatial network graph overlay plot. Currently supported Giotto objects. Possible values: TRUE — Use default spatial network. character string — graph name. name contains ":", part colon used spat_unit part graph name. NULL (default) — graph overlay. graph data retrieved via GiottoClass::getSpatialNetwork(). shape Numeric. point shape (ggplot2 shape aesthetic). Default: 16 (filled circle). See https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/aes_linetype_size_shape.html full shape palette. legend.position Character. Legend position. One "right" (default), "left", "top", "bottom", "none". legend.direction Character. Legend direction. One "vertical" (default) "horizontal". theme theme function character string naming one. Default: "theme_box". Built-aliases (usable without namespace): "theme_box" (plotthis::theme_box()), "theme_this" (plotthis::theme_this()), "theme_blank" (ggplot2::theme_void()). ggplot2 theme can used fully qualified name (e.g., "ggplot2::theme_bw"). theme_args named list additional arguments passed theme function. Default: list(). title Character. Plot title. Default: NULL (title). subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default: NULL. xlab Character. x-axis label. Default: NULL. ylab Character. y-axis label. Default: NULL. facet_scales Character. Whether facet scales "fixed" (default), "free", "free_x", "free_y". Passed ggplot2::facet_wrap() multiple features plotted. facet_nrow Integer. Number facet rows. Default: NULL (auto-calculated). facet_ncol Integer. Number facet columns. Default: NULL (auto-calculated). facet_byrow Logical. Whether fill facets row. Default: TRUE. feat_type Character. feature type (modality) query expression values Giotto objects. Common values: "rna" (default), "dna", "protein". Ignored Seurat objects. use_overlap Logical. Giotto FOV objects, whether use pre-computed polygon-feature overlap results (GiottoClass::combineFeatureOverlapData()) instead cell-level expression. Default: FALSE. shapes_feat_type Character. feature type use extracting metadata shape filling Giotto objects. Default: "cell". shapes_alpha Numeric. Transparency (alpha) value shapes layer, 0 1. points also plotted, defaults 0.5 points remain visible top shapes; otherwise defaults 1. spat_unit Character. spatial unit query Giotto object (e.g., "cell", "subcellular"). Auto-detected NULL. Ignored Seurat objects. spat_loc_name Character. spatial locations name query Giotto object. Auto-detected available spatial locations NULL. Ignored Seurat objects. spat_enr_names Character. Spatial enrichment results names Giotto object (enrichment-based feature extraction). Ignored Seurat objects. ... Additional arguments passed underlying layer functions. Arguments dispatched prefix: image_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatImagePlot() (e.g., image_alpha, image_interpolation). masks_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatMasksPlot(). shapes_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatShapesPlot() (e.g., shapes_color, shapes_linewidth). points_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatPointsPlot() (e.g., points_size, points_alpha). prefix Arguments without recognized prefix treated points arguments, lower priority points_* arguments.

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: ggplot object representing spatial dimension plot points (optionally image/shapes layers) colored group membership. group_by features produce multiple color mappings, plot may include legend mapping via ggnewscale.

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: SpatDimPlot SpatFeaturePlot share underlying SpatPlot() engine. difference conceptual: SpatDimPlot visualizes discrete groups, SpatFeaturePlot visualizes continuous values. Giotto objects, features provided, behavior automatically switches molecule mode (group_by = "molecules"). layers argument controls rendering order. Place "image" first want tissue image background, followed "shapes" cell boundaries, "points" top.

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: FOV-based technologies (Xenium, CosMx, MERSCOPE), setting group_by = "molecules" providing features molecule names switches molecule-level rendering: individual transcript detections plotted points colored feature identity, limited nmols molecules per feature. analogous Seurat::ImageDimPlot() default behavior features set Seurat FOV object.

title: Visualize categorical groups on spatial coordinates — SpatDimPlot
text: neither group_by features set: Seurat objects FOV data, defaults group_by = "molecules" features set. Otherwise, defaults group_by = "Identity", maps active cluster identities Seurat object (.e., Seurat::Idents()). Giotto objects, defaults group_by = "molecules" features set.

what: Visualize feature expression on spatial coordinates — SpatFeaturePlot
title: Visualize feature expression on spatial coordinates — SpatFeaturePlot
text: Plot continuous feature values — gene expression, dimension reduction components, metadata columns, numeric variable — directly spatial tissue coordinates. SpatFeaturePlot() spatial analogue feature plot UMAP/t-SNE embedding: paints spot, cell, molecule expression level one features, revealing spatial organization gene activity. Multiple features automatically faceted, making easy compare spatial expression patterns across gene panel single plot. categorical grouping (e.g., cluster identity spatial coordinates), use SpatDimPlot instead.

Giotto objects, one "normalized" (default), "scaled", "raw", "counts", "custom". scale_factor Internal use. image scale factor extracted object, used map pixel tissue coordinate spaces. Automatically determined object's image data. layers character vector specifying layers include order. Possible values "image", "masks", "shapes", "points". Order matters — first element drawn first (bottom). Omit layer name disable . Default: c("image", "masks", "shapes", "points") intersected layers non-FALSE/non-NULL. flip_y Logical. Whether flip y-axis. primarily internal coordinate system alignment — Visium/Slide-seq objects default TRUE, FOV-based objects FALSE. cases need set manually. padding Numeric. Extra space added around data extent crop = TRUE. Expressed fraction data range (e.g., 0.05 adds 5\ defaults 0. object types, defaults 0 image layer present 0.05 otherwise. image_scale image scale factor name use, typically "lowres" "hires". Controls resolution stored image rendered. Analogous image.scale argument Seurat::SpatialDimPlot(). x Internal use. name x-coordinate column spatial data. Auto-detected based spatial technology ("imagerow" Visium V1, varies types). y Internal use. name y-coordinate column spatial data. Auto-detected based spatial technology ("imagecol" Visium V1, varies types). nmols Integer. Maximum number molecules plot per feature group_by = "molecules". Analogous nmols argument Seurat::ImageDimPlot(). Default: 1000. shapes_fill_by column name metadata (feature/gene name) used fill cell boundary shapes. single color string provided, shapes filled color. set, shapes defaults TRUE. graph name spatial network graph overlay plot. Currently supported Giotto objects. Possible values: TRUE — Use default spatial network. character string — graph name. name contains ":", part colon used spat_unit part graph name. NULL (default) — graph overlay. graph data retrieved via GiottoClass::getSpatialNetwork(). shape Numeric. point shape (ggplot2 shape aesthetic). Default: 16 (filled circle). See https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/aes_linetype_size_shape.html full shape palette. legend.position Character. Legend position. One "right" (default), "left", "top", "bottom", "none". legend.direction Character. Legend direction. One "vertical" (default) "horizontal". theme theme function character string naming one. Default: "theme_box". Built-aliases (usable without namespace): "theme_box" (plotthis::theme_box()), "theme_this" (plotthis::theme_this()), "theme_blank" (ggplot2::theme_void()). ggplot2 theme can used fully qualified name (e.g., "ggplot2::theme_bw"). theme_args named list additional arguments passed theme function. Default: list(). title Character. Plot title. Default: NULL (title). subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default: NULL. xlab Character. x-axis label. Default: NULL. ylab Character. y-axis label. Default: NULL. facet_scales Character. Whether facet scales "fixed" (default), "free", "free_x", "free_y". Passed ggplot2::facet_wrap() multiple features plotted. facet_nrow Integer. Number facet rows. Default: NULL (auto-calculated). facet_ncol Integer. Number facet columns. Default: NULL (auto-calculated). facet_byrow Logical. Whether fill facets row. Default: TRUE. feat_type Character. feature type (modality) query expression values Giotto objects. Common values: "rna" (default), "dna", "protein". Ignored Seurat objects. use_overlap Logical. Giotto FOV objects, whether use pre-computed polygon-feature overlap results (GiottoClass::combineFeatureOverlapData()) instead cell-level expression. Default: FALSE. shapes_feat_type Character. feature type use extracting metadata shape filling Giotto objects. Default: "cell". shapes_alpha Numeric. Transparency (alpha) value shapes layer, 0 1. points also plotted, defaults 0.5 points remain visible top shapes; otherwise defaults 1. spat_unit Character. spatial unit query Giotto object (e.g., "cell", "subcellular"). Auto-detected NULL. Ignored Seurat objects. spat_loc_name Character. spatial locations name query Giotto object. Auto-detected available spatial locations NULL. Ignored Seurat objects. spat_enr_names Character. Spatial enrichment results names Giotto object (enrichment-based feature extraction). Ignored Seurat objects. ... Additional arguments passed underlying layer functions. Arguments dispatched prefix: image_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatImagePlot() (e.g., image_alpha, image_interpolation). masks_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatMasksPlot(). shapes_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatShapesPlot() (e.g., shapes_color, shapes_linewidth). points_* Arguments passed plotthis::SpatPointsPlot() (e.g., points_size, points_alpha). prefix Arguments without recognized prefix treated points arguments, lower priority points_* arguments.

title: Internal workhorse for spatial plot rendering — SpatPlot

title: Internal workhorse for spatial plot rendering — SpatPlot
text: SpatPlot.Seurat — Inspects first image's class delegate SpatPlot.Seurat.Visium, SpatPlot.Seurat.FOV, SpatPlot.Seurat.SlideSeq. SpatPlot.Seurat.Visium — Renders 10x Visium data tissue image, spot points, optional shape/mask layers. SpatPlot.Seurat.FOV — Renders FOV-based data (Xenium, CosMx, etc.) molecule-level cell-level points, cell boundary shapes, optional registered images. SpatPlot.Seurat.SlideSeq — Renders Slide-seq bead data colored background point layer. SpatPlot.giotto — Renders spatial data Giotto objects full support images, shapes, points, molecules, spatial network graphs.

what: Prepare clonal abundance data for visualization — clonal_size_data
title: Prepare clonal abundance data for visualization — clonal_size_data
text: central data preparation function used clonal visualization functions scplotter. transforms raw scRepertoire combined TCR/BCR data (list data frames Seurat object) standardized long-format data frame suitable plotting. function performs following steps: Extracts grouping levels via get_clonal_grouping_levels(). Merges multiple grouping columns single composite .group column via merge_clonal_groupings(). Reorganizes list-format data splitting .group combining cells share group across samples. Calls scRepertoire's internal .data.wrangle() .theCall() compute clone-level counts per group. Pivots resulting wide-format table (clones × groups) long format columns CloneID, .group, count, fraction. Separates composite .group column back original grouping columns. Restores original factor levels grouping variable. output data frame contains one row per clone per group, columns grouping variable, CloneID, count (number cells), fraction (proportion cells within group).

One "" (chains combined), "TRA", "TRB", "TRG", "TRD", "IGH", "IGL", "IGK". groupings character vector column names metadata use grouping cells. define categories across clone abundances computed. order named list specifying order levels grouping variable. what: Programmatic clone selection for TCR/BCR repertoire analysis — CloneSelectors
title: Programmatic clone selection for TCR/BCR repertoire analysis — CloneSelectors
text: Clone selectors provide programmatic, expression-based system filtering selecting T cell B cell clones immune repertoire data. foundation clone-level analysis scplotter, enabling flexible clone selection without manual specification clone IDs. Clone selectors operate data frames containing clone abundance information (clone IDs paired group-level counts fractions). evaluate selection criteria — abundance thresholds, group comparisons, shared presence across conditions — return either selected clone IDs, logical indicator vector, filtered data frame. system context-aware: automatically detects whether called within dplyr pipeline, scplotter function, standalone code, adjusts default behavior accordingly. following selector functions available: top() — select n largest clones abundance sel() — select clones matching custom logical expression uniq() — select clones unique specified group shared() — select clones present specified groups gt(), ge(), lt(), le(), eq(), ne() — comparison-based selection (), () — combine multiple selector results

title: Programmatic clone selection for TCR/BCR repertoire analysis — CloneSelectors

title: Programmatic clone selection for TCR/BCR repertoire analysis — CloneSelectors
text: Clone selectors designed work data produced scRepertoire::combineTCR() scRepertoire::combineBCR(), row represents cell clone IDs stored column (default "CTaa"). within output_within arguments enable powerful subset--select patterns. Use within restrict selection pool (e.g., select top clones among CD8+ T cells), use output_within additionally filter returned results. using selectors expression strings scplotter functions (e.g., clones = "top(10, group_by = 'Sample')"), expression parsed evaluated function's data context — global environment. Group names quoted within string. Selector functions detect calling context via rlang::caller_env(). calling selectors within nested wrapper functions, context detection may fail; pass data, in_form, output explicitly cases.
